#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Configure systemd-logind to terminate idle user sessions.
# Sets StopIdleSessionSec in /etc/systemd/logind.conf so idle sessions are
# terminated after a defined period of inactivity.
#
set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/systemd/logind.conf"
IDLE_SEC="900"   # 15 minutes
KEY="StopIdleSessionSec"
DESIRED_LINE="${KEY}=${IDLE_SEC}"

# Ensure the config file exists
if [ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "${CONF_FILE}")"
    touch "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Backup once
if [ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}.bak" ]; then
    cp -p "${CONF_FILE}" "${CONF_FILE}.bak"
fi

# Remove any existing (commented or uncommented) occurrences of the key
# under [Login] or elsewhere, then ensure a single canonical entry.
tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
awk -v key="${KEY}" '
{
    line=$0
    # strip leading whitespace copy for matching
    stripped=line
    sub(/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*/, "", stripped)
    if (stripped ~ "^"key"[[:space:]]*=") {
        next
    }
    print line
}
' "${CONF_FILE}" > "${tmpfile}"

# Make sure [Login] section exists
if ! grep -q '^\[Login\]' "${tmpfile}"; then
    printf '\n[Login]\n' >> "${tmpfile}"
fi

# Insert the desired setting immediately after the [Login] header
awk -v setting="${DESIRED_LINE}" '
    BEGIN { inserted=0 }
    {
        print $0
        if (!inserted && $0 ~ /^\[Login\][[:space:]]*$/) {
            print setting
            inserted=1
        }
    }
    END {
        if (!inserted) {
            print "[Login]"
            print setting
        }
    }
' "${tmpfile}" > "${CONF_FILE}"

rm -f "${tmpfile}"

chown root:root "${CONF_FILE}"
chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"

# Apply the configuration
if systemctl is-active --quiet systemd-logind; then
    systemctl restart systemd-logind
else
    systemctl start systemd-logind || true
fi

echo "systemd-logind configured with ${DESIRED_LINE}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
REKEY_VALUE="512M 1h"

# Ensure config file exists
if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remove any RekeyLimit lines from included drop-in files that might override
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ]; then
    for f in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -qiE '^\s*RekeyLimit\s+' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E 's|^\s*RekeyLimit\s+.*$|# RekeyLimit removed by remediation|I' "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Set RekeyLimit in main sshd_config
if grep -qiE '^\s*RekeyLimit\s+' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -i -E "s|^\s*RekeyLimit\s+.*$|RekeyLimit ${REKEY_VALUE}|I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    # Insert before any Match block, or append at end
    if grep -qiE '^\s*Match\s+' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        awk -v val="RekeyLimit ${REKEY_VALUE}" '
            BEGIN { inserted = 0 }
            /^[[:space:]]*[Mm]atch[[:space:]]+/ && !inserted { print val; inserted = 1 }
            { print }
            END { if (!inserted) print val }
        ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
        mv "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    else
        echo "RekeyLimit ${REKEY_VALUE}" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

chmod 0600 "$SSHD_CONFIG"
chown root:root "$SSHD_CONFIG"

# Validate configuration
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sshd -t
fi

# Reload sshd if running
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
fi

echo "RekeyLimit set to '${REKEY_VALUE}' in ${SSHD_CONFIG}"

PARAM="net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route"
VALUE=0
CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-source-route.conf"

for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do

    sed -i -E "s|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*|${PARAM} = ${VALUE}|" "$CONF_FILE"
else

fi

# Apply at runtime

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure GNOME screensaver to activate after 15 minutes
# of inactivity, enforced system-wide and locked so users cannot override.
#

set -e

TIMEOUT_SECONDS=900

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCK_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"

DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
DCONF_LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}/00-screensaver"

# Ensure dconf is installed
if ! rpm -q dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum install -y dconf
fi

# Ensure the user profile exists and references the local database
mkdir -p "$(dirname "${DCONF_PROFILE}")"
if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE}" <<EOF
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
else
    grep -q '^user-db:user$'   "${DCONF_PROFILE}" || echo 'user-db:user'   >> "${DCONF_PROFILE}"
    grep -q '^system-db:local$' "${DCONF_PROFILE}" || echo 'system-db:local' >> "${DCONF_PROFILE}"
fi

# Ensure the directories exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}"

# Write the settings file (idempotent overwrite with fixed content)
cat > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" <<EOF
# Enforce screensaver activation after ${TIMEOUT_SECONDS} seconds of inactivity
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 ${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
EOF

chmod 0644 "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"

# Write the lock file to prevent users from overriding
cat > "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}" <<EOF
# Lock screensaver/idle settings so users cannot override
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
EOF

chmod 0644 "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"

# Update the dconf database
if command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dconf update
fi

echo "GNOME idle timeout set to ${TIMEOUT_SECONDS} seconds (15 minutes) and locked system-wide."

#!/bin/bash

CHCON_PATH="/usr/bin/chcon"

if [ ! -e "$CHCON_PATH" ]; then
    echo "chcon not found at $CHCON_PATH"
    exit 1
fi

# Determine architecture-independent rule
AUID_FILTER="auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset"
RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${CHCON_PATH} -F perm=x -F ${AUID_FILTER} -k privileged"

# Function to update or add rule in a file
update_rule_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local tmpfile
    tmpfile=$(mktemp)

    # Remove any existing rules referencing chcon path
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        grep -v -E "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${CHCON_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "$file" > "$tmpfile" || true
        cat "$tmpfile" > "$file"
    fi
    rm -f "$tmpfile"

    # Append the correct rule
    echo "$RULE" >> "$file"
}

# Ensure /etc/audit/rules.d exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Prefer privileged.rules file
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
if [ ! -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    # Check if rule exists in any other .rules file
    EXISTING=$(grep -l -E "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${CHCON_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
    if [ -n "$EXISTING" ]; then
        RULES_FILE="$EXISTING"
    fi
fi

# Remove the rule from all other files to avoid duplicates
for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    if [ "$f" != "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
        if grep -q -E "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${CHCON_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "$f"; then
            sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${CHCON_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)/d" "$f"
        fi
    fi
done

update_rule_file "$RULES_FILE"

# Also update /etc/audit/audit.rules for auditctl consistency
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES" ]; then
    if grep -q -E "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${CHCON_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "$AUDIT_RULES"; then
        sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${CHCON_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)/d" "$AUDIT_RULES"
    fi
    echo "$RULE" >> "$AUDIT_RULES"
else
    echo "$RULE" > "$AUDIT_RULES"
fi

# Reload audit rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "Audit rule for chcon configured successfully."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
KEY="module-change"

# Ensure the rules file exists
[ -f "$RULE_FILE" ] || touch "$RULE_FILE"

# Determine architecture
ARCH_BITS=$(getconf LONG_BIT)

add_rule() {
    local rule="$1"
    # Escape special regex characters for grep
    if ! grep -Fxq "$rule" "$RULE_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULE_FILE"
    fi
}

# 32-bit rule (always add on both 32 and 64 bit systems where applicable)
RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
add_rule "$RULE_B32"

# 64-bit rule only on 64-bit systems
if [ "$ARCH_BITS" -eq 64 ]; then
    RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
    add_rule "$RULE_B64"
fi

# Reload audit rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

if systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    service auditd restart || systemctl restart auditd || true
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Disable user namespaces on RHEL 8 by setting user.max_user_namespaces=0
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_PARAM="user.max_user_namespaces"
SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-user-namespaces.conf"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Create/refresh the persistent sysctl configuration file.
TMPFILE="$(mktemp)"
cat > "$TMPFILE" <<EOF
# Managed file: disables user namespace creation to reduce kernel attack surface.
# See CVE history around user namespaces for rationale.
${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}
EOF

if [[ -f "$SYSCTL_FILE" ]] && cmp -s "$TMPFILE" "$SYSCTL_FILE"; then
    echo "Persistent sysctl config already in place at ${SYSCTL_FILE}."
    rm -f "$TMPFILE"
else
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "$TMPFILE" "$SYSCTL_FILE"
    rm -f "$TMPFILE"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Disable user namespaces on RHEL 8 by setting user.max_user_namespaces=0
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_PARAM="user.max_user_namespaces"
SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-user-namespaces.conf"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Create/refresh the persistent sysctl configuration file.
TMPFILE="$(mktemp)"
cat > "$TMPFILE" <<EOF
# Managed file: disables user namespace creation to reduce kernel attack surface.
# See CVE history around user namespaces for rationale.
${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}
EOF

if [[ -f "$SYSCTL_FILE" ]] && cmp -s "$TMPFILE" "$SYSCTL_FILE"; then
    echo "Persistent sysctl config already in place at ${SYSCTL_FILE}."
    rm -f "$TMPFILE"
else
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "$TMPFILE" "$SYSCTL_FILE"
    rm -f "$TMPFILE"
    echo "Wrote persistent sysctl config to ${SYSCTL_FILE}."
fi

# Remove any conflicting settings in other sysctl config locations.
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
    [[ "$f" == "$SYSCTL_FILE" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Commenting out conflicting ${SYSCTL_PARAM} entries in ${f}."
        sed -i -E "s|^(\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*)$|# \1  # disabled by hardening script|g" "$f" || true
    fi
done

# Apply the setting immediately.
current_val="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")"
if [[ "${current_val}" != "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]]; then
    sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null
    echo "Runtime kernel setting updated: ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE} (was ${current_val})."
else
    echo "Runtime kernel setting already ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}."
fi

# Reload the full sysctl configuration to ensure consistency.
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Final verification.
final_val="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")"
if [[ "${final_val}" == "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${final_val} (user namespaces disabled)."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to set ${SYSCTL_PARAM} to ${SYSCTL_VALUE}; current value is ${final_val}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Generate an initial USBGuard policy from currently connected
# USB devices so that legitimate devices are not blocked after enabling
# USBGuard on RHEL 8. Idempotent: only generates the policy if it does
# not already exist (or is empty), and ensures the service is enabled.
#
set -o pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
RULES_DIR="/etc/usbguard"

# Ensure USBGuard is installed
if ! rpm -q usbguard >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing usbguard package..."
    if ! dnf install -y usbguard; then
        echo "ERROR: failed to install usbguard" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure the configuration directory exists
if [ ! -d "$RULES_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"
    chmod 0750 "$RULES_DIR"
fi

# Determine whether a non-empty policy already exists.
# A policy is considered "in place" if the file exists and contains at
# least one non-comment, non-empty line.
policy_present=0
if [ -s "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -Ev '^[[:space:]]*(#|$)' "$RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        policy_present=1
    fi
fi

if [ "$policy_present" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Generating initial USBGuard policy from currently connected devices..."

    # Generate a policy reflecting currently connected devices.
    tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
    if ! usbguard generate-policy > "$tmpfile" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ERROR: 'usbguard generate-policy' failed" >&2
        rm -f "$tmpfile"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Backup an existing (empty) rules file, if any.
    if [ -e "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
        cp -a "$RULES_FILE" "${RULES_FILE}.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S).bak" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    install -o root -g root -m 0600 "$tmpfile" "$RULES_FILE"
    rm -f "$tmpfile"
else
    echo "Existing USBGuard policy found at $RULES_FILE; leaving in place."
fi

# Ensure correct ownership/permissions regardless
chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0600 "$RULES_FILE"

# Ensure the usbguard service is enabled and running so the policy
# persists across reboots.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable usbguard.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    if systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard.service; then
        # Reload to apply any updated ruleset without disrupting session
        systemctl reload usbguard.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            systemctl restart usbguard.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        systemctl start usbguard.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

echo "USBGuard remediation complete."
exit 0

#
# Configure auditd to log every execution of /usr/sbin/usermod
# by users with loginuid >= 1000, tagged with key "privileged".
#

set -u

RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
BINARY="/usr/sbin/usermod"

# Determine architecture-specific rule(s)
ARCH_RULES=()
if [ "$(getconf LONG_BIT)" = "64" ]; then
    ARCH_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F path=${BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged")
else
    ARCH_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F path=${BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged")
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "$RULE_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULE_FILE"

# Remove any pre-existing rules referencing this binary to avoid duplicates
# (only in our managed file and in any other file under rules.d)
for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    # Remove any line that mentions the usermod binary path in an audit rule
    sed -i "\|[[:space:]]${BINARY}\([[:space:]]\|$\)|d" "$f"
done

# Also clean audit.rules (static) if present
if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]; then
    sed -i "\|[[:space:]]${BINARY}\([[:space:]]\|$\)|d" /etc/audit/audit.rules
fi

# Append the correct rule(s) to our managed rules file
for r in "${ARCH_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qF -- "$r" "$RULE_FILE"; then
        echo "$r" >> "$RULE_FILE"
    fi
done

# Also add rule to /etc/audit/audit.rules for augenrules-independent loaders
if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]; then
    for r in "${ARCH_RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! grep -qF -- "$r" /etc/audit/audit.rules; then
            echo "$r" >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
        fi
    done
fi

# Reload the audit rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Load rules from audit.rules as fallback
    auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Load rules from audit.rules as fallback
    auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0
```

#!/bin/bash
#
# Configure auditd rules to detect setuid/setgid privilege escalation
# via execve syscalls where euid != uid or egid != gid.
#

set -u

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULE_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/privileged-execve.rules"
LIVE_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Define the required rules
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S execve -C uid!=euid -F euid=0 -k setuid"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -C uid!=euid -F euid=0 -k setuid"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S execve -C gid!=egid -F egid=0 -k setgid"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -C gid!=egid -F egid=0 -k setgid"
)

# Remove any pre-existing execve rules with these comparators from all rules files
# to avoid duplicates, then rewrite our dedicated file.
for f in "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "$RULE_FILE" ] && continue
    # Remove matching lines in-place (only fully-matching semantic rules)
    sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit.*-S[[:space:]]+execve.*-C[[:space:]]+(uid!=euid|gid!=egid).*-F[[:space:]]+(euid|egid)=0/d' "$f"
done

# Write out (or overwrite) the dedicated rule file idempotently
{
    echo "## Audit rules to detect setuid/setgid privilege escalation via execve"
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        echo "$rule"
    done
} > "${RULE_FILE}"

chmod 0640 "${RULE_FILE}"
chown root:root "${RULE_FILE}"

# Also ensure the rules appear in the compiled /etc/audit/audit.rules if present
if [ -f "${LIVE_RULES}" ]; then
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! grep -qF -- "${rule}" "${LIVE_RULES}"; then
            echo "${rule}" >> "${LIVE_RULES}"
        fi
    done
fi

# Load rules into the running kernel audit subsystem
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        # shellcheck disable=SC2086
        auditctl $rule >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    done
fi

# If auditd requires a restart flag (immutable mode), inform via reboot hint
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'enabled 2'; then
        echo "Audit is in immutable mode; a reboot is required for new rules to take effect."
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Harden auditd: make the audit rule set immutable (-e 2) at load time so that
# rules cannot be modified at runtime until the system is rebooted.
#
# On RHEL 8, auditd loads rules from /etc/audit/audit.rules at startup, which
# is generated by augenrules(8) by concatenating the files in
# /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules. The "-e 2" directive must be the LAST rule
# loaded, so we place it in a file that sorts last in rules.d (99-*).

set -u
set -o pipefail

RULES_D="/etc/audit/rules.d"
IMMUTABLE_FILE="${RULES_D}/99-finalize.rules"
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Ensure the audit package/tooling is present
if ! command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "auditctl not found; ensure the audit package is installed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Make sure the rules.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${RULES_D}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D}"
    chmod 0750 "${RULES_D}"
fi

# 1) Remove any existing "-e <N>" directives (other than -e 2) from files in
#    /etc/audit/rules.d/ so that our "-e 2" is not overridden or misplaced.
if [ -d "${RULES_D}" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        # Skip our own finalizer file
        [ "${f}" = "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ] && continue
        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-9]+' "${f}" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Remove any -e lines; the finalizer file will provide -e 2.
            sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-9]+[[:space:]]*$/d' "${f}"
        fi
    done < <(find "${RULES_D}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.rules' -print0)
fi

# 2) Create/refresh the finalizer rules file that sets immutable mode.
#    Using 99- prefix ensures augenrules places this last in audit.rules.
NEW_CONTENT='## Set audit configuration immutable.
## This MUST be the last rule loaded. Requires reboot to modify audit rules.
-e 2
'

if [ ! -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}")" != "${NEW_CONTENT}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${NEW_CONTENT}" > "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
fi
chown root:root "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"

# 3) Regenerate /etc/audit/audit.rules from the rules.d fragments via augenrules.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# 4) Verify: audit.rules should end with "-e 2".
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES}" ]; then
    if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+2[[:space:]]*$' "${AUDIT_RULES}"; then
        echo "Warning: -e 2 not present in ${AUDIT_RULES} after augenrules." >&2
    fi
fi

# 5) Report current runtime status (informational; may already be immutable).
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CUR_ENABLED="$(auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | awk '/^enabled/ {print $2; exit}')"
    if [ "${CUR_ENABLED:-}" = "2" ]; then
        echo "Audit system is already in immutable mode (enabled=2)."
    else
        echo "Audit rules configured to become immutable on next load/reboot."
        echo "A reboot is required to activate immutable mode without disrupting current rules."
    fi
fi

exit 0

IMMUTABLE_FILE="${RULES_D}/99-finalize.rules"
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Ensure the audit package/tooling is present
if ! command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "auditctl not found; ensure the audit package is installed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Make sure the rules.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${RULES_D}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D}"
    chmod 0750 "${RULES_D}"
fi

# 1) Remove any existing "-e <N>" directives (other than -e 2) from files in
#    /etc/audit/rules.d/ so that our "-e 2" is not overridden or misplaced.
if [ -d "${RULES_D}" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        # Skip our own finalizer file
        [ "${f}" = "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ] && continue
        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-9]+' "${f}" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Remove any -e lines; the finalizer file will provide -e 2.
            sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-9]+[[:space:]]*$/d' "${f}"
        fi
    done < <(find "${RULES_D}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.rules' -print0)
fi

# 2) Create/refresh the finalizer rules file that sets immutable mode.
#    Using 99- prefix ensures augenrules places this last in audit.rules.
NEW_CONTENT='## Set audit configuration immutable.
## This MUST be the last rule loaded. Requires reboot to modify audit rules.
-e 2
'

if [ ! -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}")" != "${NEW_CONTENT}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${NEW_CONTENT}" > "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
fi
chown root:root "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"

# 3) Regenerate /etc/audit/audit.rules from the rules.d fragments via augenrules.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# 4) Verify: audit.rules should end with "-e 2".
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES}" ]; then
    if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+2[[:space:]]*$' "${AUDIT_RULES}"; then
        echo "Warning: -e 2 not present in ${AUDIT_RULES} after augenrules." >&2
    fi
fi

# 5) Report current runtime status (informational; may already be immutable).
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CUR_ENABLED="$(auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | awk '/^enabled/ {print $2; exit}')"
    if [ "${CUR_ENABLED:-}" = "2" ]; then
        echo "Audit system is already in immutable mode (enabled=2)."
    else
        echo "Audit rules configured to become immutable on next load/reboot."
        echo "A reboot is required to activate immutable mode without disrupting current rules."
    fi
fi

exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable IPv4 ICMP redirect sending on default interface
# Applies runtime setting and persists across reboots.

set -u

SYSCTL_KEY="net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects"
SYSCTL_VAL="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-send-redirects.conf"

# 1. Apply the setting at runtime
/sbin/sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_KEY}=${SYSCTL_VAL}" >/dev/null

# 2. Remove any conflicting/duplicate entries from common sysctl config files
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    # Skip our target file so we don't wipe it before writing
    if [ "$f" = "$PERSIST_FILE" ]; then
        continue
    fi
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_KEY}[[:space:]]*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Only modify writable files under /etc or /run
        case "$f" in
            /etc/*|/run/*)
                sed -i --follow-symlinks "/^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_KEY}[[:space:]]*=.*/d" "$f"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
done

# 3. Write persistent configuration (idempotent)
if [ ! -f "$PERSIST_FILE" ] || ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_KEY}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_VAL}[[:space:]]*$" "$PERSIST_FILE"; then
    {
        echo "# Disable sending of ICMP redirects on default IPv4 interface"
        echo "${SYSCTL_KEY} = ${SYSCTL_VAL}"
    } > "$PERSIST_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$PERSIST_FILE"
fi

# 4. Reload sysctl settings to ensure everything is consistent
/sbin/sysctl --system >/dev/null

# 5. Verify
current_val="$(/sbin/sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_KEY}" 2>/dev/null)"
if [ "$current_val" = "$SYSCTL_VAL" ]; then
    echo "Remediation successful: ${SYSCTL_KEY} = ${current_val}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Remediation FAILED: ${SYSCTL_KEY} = ${current_val} (expected ${SYSCTL_VAL})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y aide
fi

# Ensure mailx is available for sending mail
if ! rpm -q mailx &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y mailx || true
fi

CRON_FILE="/etc/crontab"
CRON_LINE="05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s \"\$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check\" root@localhost"

# Remove any existing AIDE cron entries that don't email results
if grep -E '^\s*[^#].*aide\s*(--check|--update)' "$CRON_FILE" &>/dev/null; then
    # Check if existing entry pipes to mail
    if ! grep -E '^\s*[^#].*aide.*(--check|--update).*\|.*mail' "$CRON_FILE" &>/dev/null; then
        # Remove non-mailing aide entries
        sed -i '/aide.*--check/d; /aide.*--update/d' "$CRON_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Add the cron entry if not already present
if ! grep -qF "aide --check | /bin/mail" "$CRON_FILE"; then
    echo "$CRON_LINE" >> "$CRON_FILE"
fi

# Also ensure any /etc/cron.*/ aide scripts email their output
for f in /etc/cron.daily/aide /etc/cron.weekly/aide /etc/cron.monthly/aide; do
    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
        if ! grep -q "mail" "$f"; then
            # Backup and rewrite to pipe output to mail
            cp "$f" "${f}.bak.$(date +%s)"
            cat > "$f" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
/usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost
EOF
            chmod 0755 "$f"
        fi
    fi
done

# Ensure crond is enabled and running so the job persists across reboots
systemctl enable crond &>/dev/null || true
systemctl restart crond &>/dev/null || true

echo "AIDE scheduled check with email notification to root configured."

    echo "Error: $RSYSLOG_CONF does not exist. Is rsyslog installed?" >&2
    exit 1

        echo "$FORWARD_LINE" >> "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
    fi
else
    echo "$FORWARD_LINE" >> "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog
else
    systemctl enable --now rsyslog
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Ensure pam_faillock is enforced in the auth phase of password-auth PAM stack
# on RHEL 8 to defeat brute-force password guessing attacks.
#
set -euo pipefail

PAM_FILES=(
    "/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
    "/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
)

# On RHEL 8, authselect manages /etc/pam.d/{password,system}-auth.
# Prefer authselect if in use; otherwise edit files directly.
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Enable the with-faillock feature which enforces pam_faillock in auth phase
    if ! authselect current | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock || true
    fi
    authselect apply-changes -b >/dev/null 2>&1 || authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Also ensure the pam_faillock lines are present directly in the files
# (idempotent belt-and-suspenders in case authselect is not managing them).
for pam_file in "${PAM_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$pam_file" ] || continue

    # Backup once
    if [ ! -f "${pam_file}.bak.faillock" ]; then
        cp -a "$pam_file" "${pam_file}.bak.faillock"
    fi

    tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
    cp -a "$pam_file" "$tmpfile"

    # Ensure "auth required pam_faillock.so preauth silent" is present
    # before the first pam_unix.so auth line.
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+preauth' "$tmpfile"; then
        if grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+.*\s+pam_unix\.so' "$tmpfile"; then
            awk '
                BEGIN { inserted=0 }
                {
                    if (!inserted && $1=="auth" && $0 ~ /pam_unix\.so/) {
                        print "auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent"
                        inserted=1
                    }
                    print
                }
            ' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.new" && mv "${tmpfile}.new" "$tmpfile"
        else
            printf 'auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent\n' >> "$tmpfile"
        fi
    fi

    # Ensure "auth [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail" is present
    # after the pam_unix.so auth line.
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+\[default=die\]\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+authfail' "$tmpfile"; then
        awk '
            BEGIN { inserted=0 }
            {
                print
                if (!inserted && $1=="auth" && $0 ~ /pam_unix\.so/) {
                    print "auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail"
                    inserted=1
                }
            }
        ' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.new" && mv "${tmpfile}.new" "$tmpfile"

        if ! grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+\[default=die\]\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+authfail' "$tmpfile"; then
            printf 'auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail\n' >> "$tmpfile"
        fi
    fi

    # Ensure "auth sufficient pam_faillock.so authsucc" is present after authfail
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+sufficient\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+authsucc' "$tmpfile"; then
        awk '
            BEGIN { inserted=0 }
            {
                print
                if (!inserted && $1=="auth" && $0 ~ /pam_faillock\.so/ && $0 ~ /authfail/) {
                    print "auth        sufficient                                   pam_faillock.so authsucc"
                    inserted=1
                }
            }
        ' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.new" && mv "${tmpfile}.new" "$tmpfile"
    fi

    # Ensure account phase has pam_faillock too
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*account\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so' "$tmpfile"; then
        if grep -Pq '^\s*account\s+.*\s+pam_unix\.so' "$tmpfile"; then
            awk '
                BEGIN { inserted=0 }
                {
                    if (!inserted && $1=="account" && $0 ~ /pam_unix\.so/) {
                        print "account     required                                     pam_faillock.so"
                        inserted=1
                    }
                    print
                }
            ' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.new" && mv "${tmpfile}.new" "$tmpfile"
        else
            printf 'account     required                                     pam_faillock.so\n' >> "$tmpfile"
        fi
    fi

    if ! cmp -s "$tmpfile" "$pam_file"; then
        cat "$tmpfile" > "$pam_file"
    fi
    rm -f "$tmpfile" "${tmpfile}.new" 2>/dev/null || true
done

# Ensure /etc/security/faillock.conf has sane defaults for persistence
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
if [ -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*deny\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "deny = 3" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*unlock_time\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "unlock_time = 600" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*fail_interval\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*dir\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "dir = /var/run/faillock" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

echo "pam_faillock account lockout enforcement is in place."

        if ! grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+\[default=die\]\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+authfail' "$tmpfile"; then
            printf 'auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail\n' >> "$tmpfile"
        fi
    fi

    # Ensure "auth sufficient pam_faillock.so authsucc" is present after authfail
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*auth\s+sufficient\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+authsucc' "$tmpfile"; then
        awk '
            BEGIN { inserted=0 }
            {
                print
                if (!inserted && $1=="auth" && $0 ~ /pam_faillock\.so/ && $0 ~ /authfail/) {
                    print "auth        sufficient                                   pam_faillock.so authsucc"
                    inserted=1
                }
            }
        ' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.new" && mv "${tmpfile}.new" "$tmpfile"
    fi

    # Ensure account phase has pam_faillock too
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*account\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so' "$tmpfile"; then
        if grep -Pq '^\s*account\s+.*\s+pam_unix\.so' "$tmpfile"; then
            awk '
                BEGIN { inserted=0 }
                {
                    if (!inserted && $1=="account" && $0 ~ /pam_unix\.so/) {
                        print "account     required                                     pam_faillock.so"
                        inserted=1
                    }
                    print
                }
            ' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.new" && mv "${tmpfile}.new" "$tmpfile"
        else
            printf 'account     required                                     pam_faillock.so\n' >> "$tmpfile"
        fi
    fi

    if ! cmp -s "$tmpfile" "$pam_file"; then
        cat "$tmpfile" > "$pam_file"
    fi
    rm -f "$tmpfile" "${tmpfile}.new" 2>/dev/null || true
done

# Ensure /etc/security/faillock.conf has sane defaults for persistence
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
if [ -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*deny\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "deny = 3" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*unlock_time\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "unlock_time = 600" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*fail_interval\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    if ! grep -Pq '^\s*dir\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "dir = /var/run/faillock" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

echo "pam_faillock account lockout enforcement is in place."

if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || true

# Ensure rule is also in /etc/audit/audit.rules for the direct loading mechanism

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: ensure all non-root local filesystems are mounted with nodev
# so that character/block device nodes on those filesystems are not honored.
#

set -u

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
BACKUP="/etc/fstab.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Filesystem types considered "local" for this remediation.
LOCAL_FS_REGEX='^(ext2|ext3|ext4|xfs|btrfs|jfs|reiserfs|vfat|ntfs|f2fs)$'

# Pseudo/virtual filesystem types that must be skipped even if listed.
PSEUDO_FS_REGEX='^(proc|sysfs|devtmpfs|devpts|tmpfs|cgroup|cgroup2|mqueue|debugfs|tracefs|configfs|securityfs|pstore|bpf|autofs|rpc_pipefs|fusectl|hugetlbfs|nsfs|binfmt_misc|selinuxfs|efivarfs|overlay|squashfs|iso9660|nfs|nfs4|cifs|smbfs|fuse.*)$'

if [ ! -w "$FSTAB" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $FSTAB is not writable. Run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

cp -a "$FSTAB" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $FSTAB to $BACKUP"

TMP="$(mktemp)"

# Add nodev to eligible fstab entries.
awk -v pseudo="$PSEUDO_FS_REGEX" -v local_fs="$LOCAL_FS_REGEX" '
BEGIN { OFS="\t" }
{
    line = $0

    # Preserve blank lines and comments as-is.
    if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ || line ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) {
        print line
        next
    }

    n = split(line, f, /[[:space:]]+/)
    # A valid fstab record has at least 4 fields (device, mount, type, opts).
    if (n < 4) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Determine field indices ignoring leading empties from split.
    # awk split on regex will not create leading empty fields in gawk unless the
    # line starts with whitespace. Handle that.
    start = 1
    if (f[1] == "") start = 2

    device = f[start]
    mount  = f[start+1]
    fstype = f[start+2]
    opts   = f[start+3]

    # Skip root filesystem.
    if (mount == "/") {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Skip swap.
    if (fstype == "swap") {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Skip pseudo/network filesystems.
    if (fstype ~ pseudo) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Only act on recognized local filesystems.
    if (fstype !~ local_fs) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Check if nodev already present.
    has_nodev = 0
    m = split(opts, o, ",")
    for (i = 1; i <= m; i++) {
        if (o[i] == "nodev") { has_nodev = 1; break }
    }

    if (has_nodev) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Append nodev to options.
    if (opts == "defaults" || opts == "") {
        new_opts = (opts == "" ? "defaults" : opts) ",nodev"
    } else {
        new_opts = opts ",nodev"
    }

    f[start+3] = new_opts

    # Reconstruct line preserving original spacing loosely with tabs.
    out = f[start]
    for (i = start+1; i <= (start + n - start); i++) {
        if (i > n) break
        out = out "\t" f[i]
    }
    print out
}
' "$FSTAB" > "$TMP"

if ! cmp -s "$FSTAB" "$TMP"; then
    cat "$TMP" > "$FSTAB"
    echo "Updated $FSTAB with nodev on non-root local filesystems."
else
    echo "No changes needed in $FSTAB."
fi
rm -f "$TMP"

# Remount currently mounted non-root local filesystems to apply nodev live.
while read -r src mnt fstype opts _; do
    [ "$mnt" = "/" ] && continue
    [ -z "$fstype" ] && continue

    if ! echo "$fstype" | grep -Eq "$LOCAL_FS_REGEX"; then
        continue
    fi
    if echo "$fstype" | grep -Eq "$PSEUDO_FS_REGEX"; then
        continue
    fi

    # Verify fstab now lists this mount with nodev before remounting.
    fstab_opts="$(awk -v m="$mnt" '
        !/^[[:space:]]*#/ && NF >= 4 {
            if ($2 == m) { print $4; exit }
        }' "$FSTAB")"

    if [ -z "$fstab_opts" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    if echo ",$fstab_opts," | grep -q ',nodev,'; then
        # Check current mount options.
        current_opts="$(findmnt -n -o OPTIONS --target "$mnt" 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if echo ",$current_opts," | grep -q ',nodev,'; then
            echo "Already mounted with nodev: $mnt"
        else
            echo "Remounting $mnt with nodev..."
            if mount -o remount "$mnt"; then
                echo "  Remounted $mnt successfully."
            else
                echo "  WARNING: failed to remount $mnt; changes will apply on reboot." >&2
            fi
        fi
    fi
done < <(findmnt -rn -o SOURCE,TARGET,FSTYPE,OPTIONS)

echo "Remediation complete."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LIMITS_DIR="/etc/security/limits.d"
LIMITS_FILE="${LIMITS_DIR}/20-maxlogins.conf"
MAIN_LIMITS="/etc/security/limits.conf"

mkdir -p "${LIMITS_DIR}"

# Remove any existing maxlogins entries in the main limits.conf to avoid conflicts
if [ -f "${MAIN_LIMITS}" ]; then
    sed -i.bak -E '/^[[:space:]]*\*[[:space:]]+(hard|-)[[:space:]]+maxlogins[[:space:]]+/d' "${MAIN_LIMITS}"
fi

# Remove any other conflicting maxlogins entries in limits.d (excluding our target file)
for f in "${LIMITS_DIR}"/*.conf; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "${LIMITS_FILE}" ] && continue
    if grep -qE '^\s*\*\s+(hard|-)\s+maxlogins\s+' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i.bak -E '/^\s*\*\s+(hard|-)\s+maxlogins\s+/d' "$f"
    fi
done

# Write our idempotent limits file
cat > "${LIMITS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Enforce per-user concurrent login session cap to mitigate DoS from runaway logins
*   hard    maxlogins   10
EOF

chown root:root "${LIMITS_FILE}"
chmod 0644 "${LIMITS_FILE}"

# Verify
if grep -qE '^\*\s+hard\s+maxlogins\s+10\s*$' "${LIMITS_FILE}"; then
    echo "maxlogins limit successfully set to 10 in ${LIMITS_FILE}"
else
    echo "Failed to configure maxlogins limit" >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

chown root:root "${LIMITS_FILE}"
chmod 0644 "${LIMITS_FILE}"

    echo "maxlogins limit successfully set to 10 in ${LIMITS_FILE}"
else
    echo "Failed to configure maxlogins limit" >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/bash
set -eu

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCK_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
PROFILE_FILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"

mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}"
mkdir -p "$(dirname "${PROFILE_FILE}")"

# Ensure dconf user profile exists and references the local database
if [ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${PROFILE_FILE}" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
else
    grep -q '^user-db:user$' "${PROFILE_FILE}" || echo "user-db:user" >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    grep -q '^system-db:local$' "${PROFILE_FILE}" || echo "system-db:local" >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
fi

# Configure the setting to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot via GNOME
SETTING_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-disable-ctrl-alt-del"
cat > "${SETTING_FILE}" <<'EOF'
[org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]
logout=''
EOF

# Lock the setting so users cannot override it
LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}/00-disable-ctrl-alt-del"
cat > "${LOCK_FILE}" <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout
EOF

# Update the dconf database
dconf update

#!/bin/bash

set -u

ARCH_B64="b64"
ARCH_B32="b32"
KEY="perm_mod"
SYSCALL="lsetxattr"

# Files
AUGEN_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/31-privileged.rules"
AUDITCTL_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

add_rule_to_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local arch="$2"
    local syscall="$3"
    local key="$4"

    # Check if a rule already exists for this arch and syscall with proper auid filters
    # Rule for auid>=1000
    local rule_user="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${syscall} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${key}"
    local rule_root="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${syscall} -F auid=0 -F key=${key}"

    # Remove any existing lines for this syscall+arch to make it idempotent
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Look for existing matching rules; if not present, add
        if ! grep -Pq -- "^-a[[:space:]]+always,exit.*-F[[:space:]]+arch=${arch}(\s|,).*(-S\s+\S*\b${syscall}\b|\bS\s+${syscall}\b)" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
            :
        fi
    fi

    # Simpler approach: ensure both rules exist verbatim (with key= or -k form matched)
    if ! grep -Eq -- "^-a[[:space:]]+always,exit.*arch=${arch}.*(-S[[:space:]]+|,)${syscall}([[:space:]]|,).*auid>=1000.*(key=${key}|-k[[:space:]]+${key})" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$rule_user" >> "$file"
    fi

    if ! grep -Eq -- "^-a[[:space:]]+always,exit.*arch=${arch}.*(-S[[:space:]]+|,)${syscall}([[:space:]]|,).*auid=0.*(key=${key}|-k[[:space:]]+${key})" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$rule_root" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Ensure files exist
touch "$AUGEN_FILE"
touch "$AUDITCTL_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$AUGEN_FILE" "$AUDITCTL_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true

for f in "$AUGEN_FILE" "$AUDITCTL_FILE"; do
    add_rule_to_file "$f" "$ARCH_B64" "$SYSCALL" "$KEY"
    add_rule_to_file "$f" "$ARCH_B32" "$SYSCALL" "$KEY"
done

# Reload rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl restart auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

MODULE="firewire-core"
CONF="/etc/modprobe.d/${MODULE}.conf"

mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

# Ensure blacklist entry
if ! grep -qE "^\s*blacklist\s+${MODULE}\s*$" "$CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE}" >> "$CONF"
fi

# Ensure install override to prevent on-demand loading
if ! grep -qE "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\s+/bin/(true|false)\s*$" "$CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "install ${MODULE} /bin/false" >> "$CONF"
fi

# Unload the module if currently loaded
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "firewire_core"; then
    # Unload dependent modules first
    for dep in firewire_ohci firewire_sbp2 firewire_net; do
        if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "$dep"; then
            modprobe -r "$dep" 2>/dev/null || rmmod "$dep" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done
    modprobe -r firewire_core 2>/dev/null || rmmod firewire_core 2>/dev/null || true
fi

chmod 0644 "$CONF"

echo "FireWire (${MODULE}) support has been disabled."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable vsyscall kernel mapping on RHEL 8
# Sets "vsyscall=none" in kernel boot parameters for all installed kernels
# and ensures future kernels inherit the setting.

set -euo pipefail

PARAM="vsyscall=none"
GRUB_DEFAULT="/etc/default/grub"

# Ensure we run as root
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# 1. Apply parameter to all existing kernel boot entries via grubby
if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Remove any existing vsyscall=... to avoid duplicates/conflicts
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --remove-args="vsyscall" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${PARAM}"
else
    echo "grubby not found; cannot update existing kernel entries." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# 2. Ensure future kernels inherit the setting via /etc/default/grub
if [[ -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT}" ]]; then
    # Ensure GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line exists
    if ! grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"; then
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
    else
        # Strip any existing vsyscall=... token
        sed -ri 's/(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=".*)\bvsyscall=[^[:space:]"]*[[:space:]]*(.*")/\1\2/' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
        # Clean up any resulting double spaces or trailing spaces inside quotes
        sed -ri 's/(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")[[:space:]]+/\1/' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
        sed -ri 's/[[:space:]]+"$/"/' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
        sed -ri 's/[[:space:]]{2,}/ /g' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"

        # Append the parameter inside the quoted value
        if ! grep -E '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=".*\bvsyscall=none\b.*"' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}" >/dev/null; then
            sed -ri "s/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\")(.*)(\")/\1\2 ${PARAM}\3/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
            # Fix case where value was empty (leading space)
            sed -ri "s/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\") +/\1/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
        fi
    fi
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" > "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
fi

# 3. Regenerate grub configuration to persist the change
if [[ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]]; then
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

if command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1 && [[ -f "${GRUB_CFG}" ]]; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "${GRUB_CFG}"
fi

echo "vsyscall=none has been applied to all installed kernels and set as default for future kernels."
echo "A reboot is required for the change to take effect."

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
COMMAND_PATH="/usr/sbin/setsebool"
RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${COMMAND_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged"

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    chmod 0640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Remove any existing rules for setsebool to avoid duplicates/conflicts
if grep -qE "^[^#]*path=${COMMAND_PATH}[[:space:]]" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i "\|path=${COMMAND_PATH}[[:space:]]|d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Also check other rules files for existing entries and remove them to consolidate
for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
    [ "$f" = "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ] && continue
    if [ -f "$f" ] && grep -qE "path=${COMMAND_PATH}[[:space:]]" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "\|path=${COMMAND_PATH}[[:space:]]|d" "$f"
    fi
done

# Add the correct rule
echo "$RULE" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Also ensure it's present in /etc/audit/audit.rules if that's used directly
if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]; then
    if grep -qE "path=${COMMAND_PATH}[[:space:]]" /etc/audit/audit.rules 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "\|path=${COMMAND_PATH}[[:space:]]|d" /etc/audit/audit.rules
    fi
fi

# Reload audit rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# If auditd needs restart and is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

    echo "blacklist ${MODULE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure install override to prevent loading
if ! grep -Eq "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\s+/bin/(true|false)\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "install ${MODULE} /bin/false" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Unload the module if currently loaded
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Rebuild initramfs so the change is enforced early at boot (best-effort)
if command -v dracut >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dracut -f || true
fi

exit 0
```

# Remediation: Set restrictive umask (077) for C shell users on RHEL 8

set -o pipefail

CSH_FILE="/etc/csh.cshrc"
UMASK_VALUE="077"

# Ensure the file exists
if [ ! -f "$CSH_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$CSH_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$CSH_FILE"
    chown root:root "$CSH_FILE"
fi

# Check if a umask line exists in the file
if grep -qE '^\s*umask\s+[0-7]+' "$CSH_FILE"; then
    # Replace existing umask line(s) with the desired value
    sed -i -E "s/^\s*umask\s+[0-7]+.*/umask ${UMASK_VALUE}/" "$CSH_FILE"
else
    # Append umask setting
    printf '\numask %s\n' "$UMASK_VALUE" >> "$CSH_FILE"
fi

# Also handle /etc/csh.login if present, for login shells
CSH_LOGIN="/etc/csh.login"
if [ -f "$CSH_LOGIN" ]; then
    if grep -qE '^\s*umask\s+[0-7]+' "$CSH_LOGIN"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^\s*umask\s+[0-7]+.*/umask ${UMASK_VALUE}/" "$CSH_LOGIN"
    else
        printf '\numask %s\n' "$UMASK_VALUE" >> "$CSH_LOGIN"
    fi
fi

# Verify
echo "Current umask settings in csh configuration files:"
grep -E '^\s*umask' "$CSH_FILE" 2>/dev/null
[ -f "$CSH_LOGIN" ] && grep -E '^\s*umask' "$CSH_LOGIN" 2>/dev/null

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable IPv4 packet forwarding on RHEL 8
# Ensures net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0 and net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
# both at runtime and persistently across reboots.

set -o nounset
set -o pipefail

SYSCTL_DROPIN_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
REMEDIATION_FILE="${SYSCTL_DROPIN_DIR}/60-disable-ipv4-forwarding.conf"

PARAMS=(
    "net.ipv4.ip_forward"
    "net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding"
)

# Ensure sysctl drop-in directory exists
if [ ! -d "${SYSCTL_DROPIN_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_DROPIN_DIR}"
fi

# Remove any existing settings from other sysctl config files that enable forwarding
# so our setting is authoritative.
SYSCTL_CONF_FILES=(
    "/etc/sysctl.conf"
    "/run/sysctl.d/"*.conf
    "/etc/sysctl.d/"*.conf
    "/usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/"*.conf
    "/usr/lib/sysctl.d/"*.conf
    "/lib/sysctl.d/"*.conf
)

for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
    for f in "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
        # Skip glob non-matches and our own file
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        [ "$f" = "${REMEDIATION_FILE}" ] && continue
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i --follow-symlinks -E "/^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/d" "$f"
        fi
    done
done

# Write authoritative remediation drop-in
{
    echo "# Disable IPv4 packet forwarding (host is not a router)"
    for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
        echo "${param} = 0"
    done
} > "${REMEDIATION_FILE}"

chmod 0644 "${REMEDIATION_FILE}"
chown root:root "${REMEDIATION_FILE}"

# Apply at runtime
for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
    sysctl -w "${param}=0" >/dev/null
done

# Reload all sysctl settings to ensure persistence config is active
sysctl --system >/dev/null

# Verify
rc=0
for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
    current="$(sysctl -n "${param}" 2>/dev/null || echo "unset")"
    if [ "${current}" != "0" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: ${param} is '${current}', expected '0'" >&2
        rc=1
    fi
done

exit "${rc}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BINARY="/usr/libexec/openssh/ssh-keysign"
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
RUNTIME_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Determine architecture bit-ness for audit rule
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "x86_64" || "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" || "$(uname -m)" == "ppc64le" || "$(uname -m)" == "s390x" ]]; then
    ARCH="b64"
else
    ARCH="b32"
fi

RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged"

# Ensure rules.d file exists
touch "${RULES_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}"

# Remove any existing (possibly malformed) rules referencing this binary with different keys/fields
# but only lines that match the ssh-keysign path
if grep -q "${BINARY}" "${RULES_FILE}"; then
    # Remove existing matching lines to rewrite cleanly
    sed -i "\|${BINARY}|d" "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Append the correct rule
echo "${RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"

# Also ensure it is present in /etc/audit/audit.rules (some setups load from here directly)
if [[ -f "${RUNTIME_RULES}" ]]; then
    if grep -q "${BINARY}" "${RUNTIME_RULES}"; then
        sed -i "\|${BINARY}|d" "${RUNTIME_RULES}"
    fi
    echo "${RULE}" >> "${RUNTIME_RULES}"
fi

# Load rules: prefer augenrules if available, else auditctl
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

# Ensure the rule is active in the running kernel audit subsystem
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "${BINARY}"; then
        auditctl ${RULE} || true
    fi
fi

# If auditd is running, try reload (in case it is in immutable mode this will noop)
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
    service auditd reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

set -eu

SUDOERS_DIR="/etc/sudoers.d"
CONF_FILE="${SUDOERS_DIR}/zz-disable-rootpw-targetpw-runaspw"

mkdir -p "${SUDOERS_DIR}"
chmod 0750 "${SUDOERS_DIR}"

# Remove any existing rootpw/targetpw/runaspw settings from /etc/sudoers and /etc/sudoers.d/*
clean_file() {
    local f="$1"
    [ -f "$f" ] || return 0
    # Skip our own file for now
    if [ "$f" = "$CONF_FILE" ]; then
        return 0
    fi
    # Remove lines that set (positively) rootpw, targetpw, or runaspw as Defaults
    # Match lines like: Defaults rootpw, Defaults    targetpw, Defaults:user runaspw, etc.
    # Only remove enabling forms (not the !form)
    sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*Defaults([[:space:]]|:|@|>|!)*[^!#]*[[:space:],]*(rootpw|targetpw|runaspw)([[:space:]]*$|[[:space:]]*,)/d' "$f" 2>/dev/null || true

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure all files and directories within each local
# interactive user's home directory are group-owned by a group of which
# that user is a member.
#

set -u

# Determine UID range for interactive users from /etc/login.defs
UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN\s+/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs)
UID_MAX=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MAX\s+/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs)
UID_MIN=${UID_MIN:-1000}
UID_MAX=${UID_MAX:-60000}

# Iterate over local interactive users from /etc/passwd
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid gid home shell; do
    # Skip if UID not in interactive range
    [ "$uid" -ge "$UID_MIN" ] || continue
    [ "$uid" -le "$UID_MAX" ] || continue

    # Skip system/service accounts with non-interactive shells
    case "$shell" in
        */nologin|*/false|*/sync|*/halt|*/shutdown|"")
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Skip if home directory does not exist or is not a directory
    [ -d "$home" ] || continue

    # Skip common non-user home directories
    case "$home" in
        /|/root|/dev/null|/sbin/nologin|/bin/*|/sbin/*|/usr/*|/var/*|/tmp|/dev|/proc|/sys)
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Get the list of GIDs the user is a member of (primary + supplementary)
    user_gids=$(id -G "$username" 2>/dev/null)
    [ -n "$user_gids" ] || continue

    # Build a lookup of the user's group IDs
    declare -A member_gids=()
    for g in $user_gids; do
        member_gids[$g]=1
    done

    # Find all files/dirs under $home whose group is not in user's groups
    # Use -print0 to safely handle unusual filenames
    while IFS= read -r -d '' path; do
        cur_gid=$(stat -c '%g' "$path" 2>/dev/null) || continue
        if [ -z "${member_gids[$cur_gid]:-}" ]; then
            # Change group ownership to the user's primary group
            chgrp --no-dereference "$gid" "$path" 2>/dev/null || \
                chgrp -h "$gid" "$path" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done < <(find "$home" -xdev -print0 2>/dev/null)

    unset member_gids
done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# The syscall that changes DAC permissions of an already-open file is fchmod
# Also fchmodat changes permissions but may or may not require open file
# Standard STIG/CIS rule covers chmod, fchmod, fchmodat for perm_mod key
# Question specifies "already-open file" -> fchmod specifically

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
KEY="perm_mod"

# Determine architecture
ARCH_64=false
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "x86_64" || "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" || "$(uname -m)" == "ppc64le" || "$(uname -m)" == "s390x" ]]; then
    ARCH_64=true
fi

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Define the rules
RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchmod -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchmod -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"

# Create the rules file if it doesn't exist
touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Function to add rule if not present (idempotent)
add_rule() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    # Check across all rules.d files and audit.rules
    local found=false
    for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules /etc/audit/audit.rules; do
        [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
        # Normalize: check for a line with same arch and fchmod syscall and perm_mod key and auid>=1000
        if grep -Pq "^\s*-a\s+always,exit\s+.*-F\s+arch=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oP 'arch=\K\w+').*-S\s+fchmod.*-F\s+auid>=1000.*-F\s+auid!=(unset|-1|4294967295).*-k\s+${KEY}" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            found=true
            break
        fi
    done
    if ! $found; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Also handle auid!=-1 or auid!=4294967295 as equivalents to unset
if $ARCH_64; then
    add_rule "$RULE_B32" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    add_rule "$RULE_B64" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
else
    add_rule "$RULE_B32" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Load the new rules
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

# Check if reboot is needed for auditd
if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -q "enabled 2"; then
    echo "Audit is in immutable mode. Reboot required to apply new rules."
fi

exit 0

    echo "Audit is in immutable mode. Reboot required to apply new rules."

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure OpenSSH server MACs are governed by the system-wide
# crypto policy (no local override in /etc/ssh/sshd_config or drop-ins).
#
# On RHEL 8, sshd is configured via /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config
# which is included by /etc/sysconfig/sshd (CRYPTO_POLICY variable). Any local
# 'MACs' directive in sshd_config or its drop-ins overrides this and must be removed.

set -o pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
SYSCONFIG_SSHD="/etc/sysconfig/sshd"
CRYPTO_BACKEND="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"

changed=0

# 1. Remove any explicit 'MACs' directive from /etc/ssh/sshd_config
if [ -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    if grep -iEq '^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        sed -i.bak -E '/^[[:space:]]*[Mm][Aa][Cc][Ss][[:space:]].*/d' "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        changed=1
        echo "Removed local MACs override from $SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

# 2. Remove any explicit 'MACs' directive from drop-in files in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/
if [ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        if grep -iEq '^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]' "$f"; then
            sed -i.bak -E '/^[[:space:]]*[Mm][Aa][Cc][Ss][[:space:]].*/d' "$f"
            changed=1
            echo "Removed local MACs override from $f"
        fi
    done < <(find "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f -print0)
fi

# 3. Ensure /etc/sysconfig/sshd does NOT opt out of the system crypto policy.
#    A commented CRYPTO_POLICY= means "use system crypto policy" (the default).
#    An uncommented empty or custom CRYPTO_POLICY= would override the policy.
if [ -f "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD" ]; then
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*CRYPTO_POLICY[[:space:]]*=' "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD"; then
        sed -i.bak -E 's/^([[:space:]]*)(CRYPTO_POLICY[[:space:]]*=.*)$/\1# \2/' "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD"
        changed=1
        echo "Commented out CRYPTO_POLICY override in $SYSCONFIG_SSHD"
    fi
fi

# 4. Ensure the crypto-policies opensshserver back-end is present.
if [ ! -e "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" ]; then
    if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Regenerating crypto policy back-ends..."
        update-crypto-policies --set "$(cat /etc/crypto-policies/config 2>/dev/null || echo DEFAULT)" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        changed=1
    fi
fi

# 5. Validate sshd configuration and reload if remediation changed anything.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if sshd -t; then
            if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd 2>/dev/null || true
            else
                service sshd reload 2>/dev/null || service sshd restart 2>/dev/null || true
            fi
            echo "sshd configuration reloaded."
        else
            echo "ERROR: sshd -t reported invalid configuration; not reloading." >&2
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
    echo "Remediation complete: SSH MACs now governed by system-wide crypto policy."
else
    echo "Already compliant: no local MACs override present; system crypto policy governs sshd."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable the Bluetooth kernel module on RHEL 8
# Prevents the module from being loaded on demand or automatically.
#

set -u

MODULE="bluetooth"
CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
CONF_FILE="${CONF_DIR}/${MODULE}-blacklist.conf"

# Ensure modprobe.d exists
if [ ! -d "${CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${CONF_DIR}"
    chmod 0755 "${CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Desired configuration content
DESIRED_CONTENT="# Disable Bluetooth kernel module (remediation)
install ${MODULE} /bin/false
blacklist ${MODULE}"

# Write configuration idempotently
if [ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${CONF_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"
    chown root:root "${CONF_FILE}"
    echo "Updated ${CONF_FILE}"
else
    echo "${CONF_FILE} already configured"
fi

# Remove any other modprobe.d entries that might re-enable bluetooth
for f in "${CONF_DIR}"/*.conf /etc/modprobe.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "${CONF_FILE}" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*install[[:space:]]+${MODULE}[[:space:]]+(/bin/true|/sbin/modprobe)" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i.bak -E "/^[[:space:]]*install[[:space:]]+${MODULE}[[:space:]]+.*/d" "$f"
        echo "Removed conflicting install directive from $f"
    fi
done

# Stop and mask the bluetooth service if present
if systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^bluetooth\.service'; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet bluetooth.service; then
        systemctl stop bluetooth.service || true
    fi
    if ! systemctl is-enabled bluetooth.service 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^masked$'; then
        systemctl disable bluetooth.service 2>/dev/null || true
        systemctl mask bluetooth.service || true
        echo "Masked bluetooth.service"
    fi
fi

# Unload the module if currently loaded (best effort; may fail if in use)
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    # Unload dependent modules first
    for dep in $(lsmod | awk -v m="${MODULE}" '$1==m {print $4}' | tr ',' ' '); do
        [ -n "$dep" ] && modprobe -r "$dep" 2>/dev/null || true
    done
    modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || echo "Warning: could not unload ${MODULE} (may require reboot)"
fi

echo "Bluetooth module remediation complete."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"

# Syscalls that change file ownership
SYSCALLS="chown fchown fchownat lchown"

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Function to add rule if not present
add_rule() {
    local rule="$1"
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
}

# 64-bit rule
RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
# 32-bit rule
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"

# Remove any existing conflicting/partial rules for these syscalls with perm_mod key to keep idempotent
# Then add proper rules
sed -i '/-F arch=b64.*chown.*perm_mod/d' "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
sed -i '/-F arch=b32.*chown.*perm_mod/d' "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

add_rule "$RULE_64"
add_rule "$RULE_32"

# Reload audit rules
if pgrep -x auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1
fi

exit 0

    local rule="$1"
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
}

# 64-bit rule
RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
# 32-bit rule
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"

# Remove any existing conflicting/partial rules for these syscalls with perm_mod key to keep idempotent
# Then add proper rules
sed -i '/-F arch=b64.*chown.*perm_mod/d' "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
sed -i '/-F arch=b32.*chown.*perm_mod/d' "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

add_rule "$RULE_64"
add_rule "$RULE_32"

# Reload audit rules
if pgrep -x auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"

# Backup fstab
cp -a "$FSTAB" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Check if /dev/shm exists in fstab
if awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" '$2 == mp && $1 !~ /^#/ {found=1} END {exit !found}' "$FSTAB"; then
    # Entry exists - ensure nosuid is in options
    awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" '
    BEGIN { OFS="\t" }
    {
        if ($1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == mp) {
            n = split($4, opts, ",")
            has_nosuid = 0
            for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
                if (opts[i] == "nosuid") has_nosuid = 1
            }
            if (!has_nosuid) {
                if ($4 == "defaults" || $4 == "") {
                    $4 = $4 ",nosuid"
                } else {
                    $4 = $4 ",nosuid"
                }
            }
        }
        print
    }' "$FSTAB" > "${FSTAB}.tmp"
    mv "${FSTAB}.tmp" "$FSTAB"
    chmod 644 "$FSTAB"
else
    # Add new entry
    echo -e "tmpfs\t/dev/shm\ttmpfs\tdefaults,nodev,nosuid,noexec\t0 0" >> "$FSTAB"
fi

# Remount /dev/shm to apply changes immediately
if mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then
    mount -o remount "$MOUNT_POINT"
else

fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure account lockout events are logged by faillock on RHEL 8.
# This configures /etc/security/faillock.conf to enable audit logging of
# account lockouts, persisting across reboots.
#

set -u

CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure the faillock.conf file exists
if [ ! -f "${CONF}" ]; then
    touch "${CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${CONF}"
    chown root:root "${CONF}"
fi

# Function to set a key = value pair (uncommented) in faillock.conf
set_faillock_option() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    # If there is an uncommented line, replace it
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${key}\b" "${file}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*${key}\b.*|${key} = ${value}|" "${file}"
    # Else if there is a commented-out line, uncomment and set it
    elif grep -Eq "^\s*#\s*${key}\b" "${file}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*#\s*${key}\b.*|${key} = ${value}|" "${file}"
    else
        # Otherwise append it
        printf '%s = %s\n' "${key}" "${value}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# Set audit so that lockouts are recorded via the audit system / syslog
set_faillock_option "audit"        ""    "${CONF}"
# Some pam_faillock builds require a key without a value for audit; ensure it is present as a bare directive as well.
# Normalize: ensure just the bare "audit" keyword line exists (no "= ") because pam_faillock treats audit as a flag.
sed -i -E 's|^\s*audit\s*=\s*$|audit|' "${CONF}"
if ! grep -Eq '^\s*audit\s*$' "${CONF}"; then
    echo "audit" >> "${CONF}"
fi
# Deduplicate any stray audit lines, keeping one
awk '!(/^\s*audit\s*$/ && seen++)' "${CONF}" > "${CONF}.tmp" && mv "${CONF}.tmp" "${CONF}"

# Ensure silent is NOT set (we want messages logged, not suppressed)
sed -i -E 's|^\s*silent\s*$|# silent|' "${CONF}"
sed -i -E 's|^\s*silent\s*=.*|# silent|' "${CONF}"

# Ensure faillock is actually enabled in the PAM stack via authselect
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_PROFILE="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk -F': ' '/Profile ID/ {print $2}')"
    if [ -n "${CURRENT_PROFILE}" ]; then
        # Enable with-faillock feature if not already enabled
        if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock || true
        fi
        authselect apply-changes -b --backup=faillock-remediation.backup >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            authselect apply-changes -b >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Fix perms
chmod 0644 "${CONF}"
chown root:root "${CONF}"

# Restore SELinux context if applicable
if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon -F "${CONF}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "Remediation complete: account lockout audit logging is enabled in ${CONF}."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_CONF_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
SYSCTL_FILE="${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}/99-disable-icmp-redirects.conf"
PARAM_ALL="net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects"
PARAM_DEFAULT="net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects"

mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}"

# Remove any existing conflicting settings in other sysctl config files
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "${SYSCTL_FILE}" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*(${PARAM_ALL}|${PARAM_DEFAULT})\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "/^\s*(${PARAM_ALL//./\\.}|${PARAM_DEFAULT//./\\.})\s*=/d" "$f"
    fi
done

# Write our authoritative config
cat > "${SYSCTL_FILE}" <<EOF
# Disable sending ICMP redirects (host is not a router)
${PARAM_ALL} = 0
${PARAM_DEFAULT} = 0
EOF

chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_FILE}"

# Apply at runtime
sysctl -w "${PARAM_ALL}=0" >/dev/null
sysctl -w "${PARAM_DEFAULT}=0" >/dev/null

# Apply to all currently existing interfaces
for iface_dir in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/*/send_redirects; do
    [ -w "$iface_dir" ] && echo 0 > "$iface_dir"
done

# Reload sysctl settings to ensure persistence config is valid
sysctl --system >/dev/null

# Verify
rc=0
current_all=$(sysctl -n "${PARAM_ALL}")
current_default=$(sysctl -n "${PARAM_DEFAULT}")
if [ "${current_all}" != "0" ] || [ "${current_default}" != "0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to disable ICMP redirects (all=${current_all}, default=${current_default})" >&2
    rc=1
fi

exit "$rc"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Configure faillock to use persistent directory for tally storage
# so that account lockout state survives reboots on RHEL 8.
#

set -o pipefail

PERSISTENT_DIR="/var/log/faillock"
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
PAM_FILES=(
    "/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
    "/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
)

# Ensure the persistent directory exists with proper permissions
if [ ! -d "$PERSISTENT_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$PERSISTENT_DIR"
fi
chown root:root "$PERSISTENT_DIR"
chmod 0751 "$PERSISTENT_DIR"

# Prefer authselect if in use
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Enable faillock features if not enabled
    if ! authselect current | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock || true
    fi
    authselect apply-changes -b >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Configure /etc/security/faillock.conf (RHEL 8 supports this)
if [ -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ] || touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    if grep -qE '^\s*#?\s*dir\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*#?\s*dir\s*=.*|dir = ${PERSISTENT_DIR}|" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        echo "dir = ${PERSISTENT_DIR}" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

# Also patch PAM stack directly in case faillock.conf 'dir' is not honored
# or authselect templates require explicit dir= arguments.
for pam_file in "${PAM_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$pam_file" ] || continue

    # For every pam_faillock.so line, ensure dir=<PERSISTENT_DIR> is present
    # and any existing dir= or file= argument is normalized.
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    awk -v d="$PERSISTENT_DIR" '
    {
        line=$0
        if (line ~ /pam_faillock\.so/) {
            # remove any existing dir= or file= arguments
            gsub(/[ \t]+dir=[^ \t]+/, "", line)
            gsub(/[ \t]+file=[^ \t]+/, "", line)
            # append dir=
            sub(/[ \t]*$/, "", line)
            line = line " dir=" d
        }
        print line
    }' "$pam_file" > "$tmp" && cat "$tmp" > "$pam_file"
    rm -f "$tmp"
done

# Reset SELinux context on the persistent directory
if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon -R "$PERSISTENT_DIR" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Ensure sssd and required packages are installed
if ! rpm -q sssd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y sssd
fi

if ! rpm -q authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y authselect
fi

# Ensure sssd.conf exists with pam_cert_auth enabled
SSSD_CONF="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf"

if [ ! -f "$SSSD_CONF" ]; then
    cat > "$SSSD_CONF" <<'EOF'
[sssd]
config_file_version = 2
services = nss, pam
domains = shadowutils

[pam]
pam_cert_auth = True

[domain/shadowutils]
id_provider = files
EOF
    chmod 600 "$SSSD_CONF"
    chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"
else
    # Ensure [pam] section exists
    if ! grep -q '^\[pam\]' "$SSSD_CONF"; then
        echo "" >> "$SSSD_CONF"
        echo "[pam]" >> "$SSSD_CONF"
        echo "pam_cert_auth = True" >> "$SSSD_CONF"
    else
        # Ensure pam_cert_auth = True is set in [pam] section
        if grep -q '^\s*pam_cert_auth\s*=' "$SSSD_CONF"; then
            sed -i 's/^\s*pam_cert_auth\s*=.*/pam_cert_auth = True/' "$SSSD_CONF"
        else
            sed -i '/^\[pam\]/a pam_cert_auth = True' "$SSSD_CONF"
        fi
    fi
    chmod 600 "$SSSD_CONF"
    chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"
fi

# Enable and start sssd
systemctl enable sssd
systemctl restart sssd || true

# Configure authselect to enable smart card authentication
CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' || echo "")

if [ -z "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]; then
    authselect select sssd with-smartcard --force
else
    # Get current features
    CURRENT_FEATURES=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -A100 "Enabled features" | tail -n +2 | sed 's/^-\s*//' | tr '\n' ' ')
    
    if ! echo "$CURRENT_FEATURES" | grep -qw "with-smartcard"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-smartcard
    fi
fi

authselect apply-changes 2>/dev/null || true

echo "Smart card authentication configured successfully."

}

    dnf install -y aide

    echo "AIDE configuration file not found at $AIDE_CONF" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# that don't already contain xattrs, and append +xattrs
# A ruleset definition line matches: WORD = something with + or containing known checks
# We identify them as lines with format: IDENTIFIER = VALUE where VALUE contains rule tokens

if [ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}.bak" ]; then
    cp -a "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.bak"
fi

BEGIN { in_rule = 0 }
{
    line = $0
    # Skip comments and empty lines
    if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ || line ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) {
        print line
        next
    }
    # Match ruleset definition: identifier = value (not a directive starting with @@ or /)
    # Ruleset lines look like: NAME = R+something  or  NAME = p+i+n+...
    if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*[^[:space:]]+/ && line !~ /^[[:space:]]*@@/) {
