```bash
        # Check if value contains ruleset tokens (has + or is a known macro/check)
        match(line, /=[[:space:]]*[^[:space:]#]+/)
        if (RSTART > 0) {
            eq_pos = index(line, "=")
            lhs = substr(line, 1, eq_pos)
            rest = substr(line, eq_pos + 1)
            # Split rest into leading whitespace, value, trailing
            n = match(rest, /[^[:space:]]+/)
            if (n > 0) {
                leading = substr(rest, 1, RSTART - 1)
                value = substr(rest, RSTART, RLENGTH)
                trailing = substr(rest, RSTART + RLENGTH)
                # Only modify if value looks like a ruleset (contains + or is known check chars)
                if (value ~ /\+/ || value ~ /^[pinugsmcaMSbHrlLXEC0-9]+$/ || value ~ /^R$|^L$|^>$|^E$/) {
                    if (value !~ /xattrs/) {
                        value = value "+xattrs"
                    }
                }
                print lhs leading value trailing
                next
            }
        }
    }
    print line
}
' "$AIDE_CONF" > "${AIDE_CONF}.tmp"

if ! cmp -s "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.tmp"; then
    mv "${AIDE_CONF}.tmp" "$AIDE_CONF"
    chmod 600 "$AIDE_CONF"
    echo "Updated $AIDE_CONF to include xattrs in rulesets."
else
    rm -f "${AIDE_CONF}.tmp"
    echo "$AIDE_CONF already has xattrs in all rulesets."
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="slub_debug=P"

# Apply to all existing kernels via grubby
grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${PARAM}"

# Ensure future kernels inherit the setting via /etc/default/grub
GRUB_DEFAULT="/etc/default/grub"
touch "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"

if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"; then
    # Extract current value
    current_line=$(grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}")
    # Get the value between quotes
    current_val=$(echo "${current_line}" | sed -E 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="(.*)"$/\1/')

    # Remove any existing slub_debug= entries
    new_val=$(echo "${current_val}" | sed -E 's/(^|[[:space:]])slub_debug=[^[:space:]]*//g' | sed -E 's/^[[:space:]]+//; s/[[:space:]]+$//; s/[[:space:]]+/ /g')

    # Append our parameter
    if [ -z "${new_val}" ]; then
        new_val="${PARAM}"
    else
        new_val="${new_val} ${PARAM}"
    fi

    # Replace the line
    sed -i -E "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${new_val}\"|" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
fi

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="slub_debug=P"

# Apply to all existing kernels via grubby
grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${PARAM}"

# Ensure future kernels inherit the setting via /etc/default/grub
GRUB_DEFAULT="/etc/default/grub"
touch "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"

if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"; then
    # Extract current value
    current_line=$(grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}")
    # Get the value between quotes
    current_val=$(echo "${current_line}" | sed -E 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="(.*)"$/\1/')

    # Remove any existing slub_debug= entries
    new_val=$(echo "${current_val}" | sed -E 's/(^|[[:space:]])slub_debug=[^[:space:]]*//g' | sed -E 's/^[[:space:]]+//; s/[[:space:]]+$//; s/[[:space:]]+/ /g')

    # Append our parameter
    if [ -z "${new_val}" ]; then
        new_val="${PARAM}"
    else
        new_val="${new_val} ${PARAM}"
    fi

    # Replace the line
    sed -i -E "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${new_val}\"|" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
fi

# Regenerate grub config for BIOS and UEFI
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG=$(find /boot/efi/EFI -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
    [ -z "${GRUB_CFG}" ] && GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

grub2-mkconfig -o "${GRUB_CFG}"

echo "slub_debug=P has been applied to all installed kernels and will persist for future kernels."
```

add_rule() {
    local rulefile="$1"
    if [ ! -f "$rulefile" ]; then
        touch "$rulefile"
    fi
    # Remove any existing watch rule on the file that has different perms/key
    if grep -Pq -- "^[\s]*-w[\s]+${FILE}[\s]+" "$rulefile"; then
        # Check if the exact rule exists
        if ! grep -Pq -- "^[\s]*-w[\s]+${FILE}[\s]+-p[\s]+wa[\s]+-k[\s]+${KEY}[\s]*$" "$rulefile"; then

            echo "$RULE" >> "$rulefile"
        fi
    else
        echo "$RULE" >> "$rulefile"
    fi
}

if [ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]; then
    add_rule "/etc/audit/rules.d/audit_rules_usergroup_modification.rules"
fi

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"

# Ensure /etc/fstab has an entry for /dev/shm with noexec option
if grep -E -q "^\s*[^#].*\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+" "$FSTAB"; then
    # Entry exists - ensure it has noexec, nosuid, nodev (add noexec at minimum)
    awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" '
    BEGIN { OFS="\t" }
    {
        if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ || NF < 4) { print; next }
        if ($2 == mp) {
            n = split($4, opts, ",")
            has_noexec = 0
            for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
                if (opts[i] == "noexec") has_noexec = 1
            }
            if (!has_noexec) {
                if ($4 == "defaults" || $4 == "") {
                    $4 = $4 ",noexec"
                } else {
                    $4 = $4 ",noexec"
                }
            }
            print
            next
        }
        print
    }
    ' "$FSTAB" > "${FSTAB}.tmp"
    mv "${FSTAB}.tmp" "$FSTAB"
    chmod 0644 "$FSTAB"
else
    # Add new entry
    printf "tmpfs\t%s\ttmpfs\tdefaults,nodev,nosuid,noexec\t0 0\n" "$MOUNT_POINT" >> "$FSTAB"
fi

# Apply to active mount
if mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then
    mount -o remount "$MOUNT_POINT"
else
    mount "$MOUNT_POINT"
fi

# Verify
if findmnt -no OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT" | grep -qw noexec; then
    echo "Successfully applied noexec to $MOUNT_POINT"
else
    echo "Failed to apply noexec to $MOUNT_POINT" >&2
    exit 1
fi
```

    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
    USE_AUGENRULES=1

    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
    USE_AUGENRULES=0

rule_exists() {
    local f="$1"
    [ -f "$f" ] || return 1
    grep -Pq "^\s*-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+.*-F\s+path=${FILE}(\s|$).*-F\s+perm=x.*-F\s+auid>=1000.*-F\s+auid!=(unset|4294967295).*-k\s+\S+" "$f"

if [ "$USE_AUGENRULES" -eq 1 ]; then
    for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        if [ "$f" != "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
            if grep -Pq "^\s*-a\s+.*-F\s+path=${FILE}(\s|$)" "$f"; then
                sed -i -E "\|^\s*-a\s+.*-F\s+path=${FILE}(\s|$).*|d" "$f"
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# Add rule if not present
if ! rule_exists "$RULES_FILE"; then
    # Remove any partial/old rule for this path in the target file
    if [ -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
        sed -i -E "\|^\s*-a\s+.*-F\s+path=${FILE}(\s|$).*|d" "$RULES_FILE"
    fi
    echo "$RULE" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"
fi

# Load the rules
if [ "$USE_AUGENRULES" -eq 1 ]; then
    /sbin/augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
    /sbin/auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PARAM="dcredit"
VALUE="-1"

# Ensure the configuration file exists
[ -f "$CONF" ] || { mkdir -p /etc/security; touch "$CONF"; }

# Remove any existing dcredit lines (including commented ones) and append the desired setting
if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*dcredit\s*=' "$CONF"; then
    sed -i -E "s|^\s*#?\s*dcredit\s*=.*|${PARAM} = ${VALUE}|" "$CONF"
else
    echo "${PARAM} = ${VALUE}" >> "$CONF"
fi

# Ensure only a single dcredit line remains (idempotency safeguard)
if [ "$(grep -Ec '^\s*dcredit\s*=' "$CONF")" -gt 1 ]; then
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    awk -v p="$PARAM" -v v="$VALUE" '
        BEGIN { done=0 }
        /^[[:space:]]*dcredit[[:space:]]*=/ {
            if (!done) { print p " = " v; done=1 }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$CONF" > "$tmp"
    cat "$tmp" > "$CONF"
    rm -f "$tmp"
fi

# Also drop any per-file overrides in /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/ that could weaken this setting
if [ -d /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eq '^\s*dcredit\s*=' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E "s|^\s*dcredit\s*=.*|${PARAM} = ${VALUE}|" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Verify
grep -E '^\s*dcredit\s*=' "$CONF" || { echo "Failed to set dcredit in $CONF" >&2; exit 1; }

echo "Password quality dcredit successfully enforced to ${VALUE} in ${CONF}"
exit 0

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX=0

fi

# Remove any existing (including commented) ClientAliveCountMax entries

echo "ClientAliveInterval ${CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
echo "ClientAliveCountMax ${CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

if [ -d /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d ]; then

        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        sed -ri '/^\s*#?\s*ClientAliveInterval\s+/d' "$f"
        sed -ri '/^\s*#?\s*ClientAliveCountMax\s+/d' "$f"
    done
fi

# Validate configuration
if sshd -t; then
    chmod 600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
else
    echo "sshd config validation failed; restoring backup" >&2
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL=600
CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX=0

# Backup config
if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" ]; then
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak"
fi

# Remove any existing (including commented) ClientAliveInterval entries
sed -ri '/^\s*#?\s*ClientAliveInterval\s+/d' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
# Remove any existing (including commented) ClientAliveCountMax entries
sed -ri '/^\s*#?\s*ClientAliveCountMax\s+/d' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Append the required settings
echo "ClientAliveInterval ${CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
echo "ClientAliveCountMax ${CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Also handle any drop-in configs that may override
if [ -d /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        sed -ri '/^\s*#?\s*ClientAliveInterval\s+/d' "$f"
        sed -ri '/^\s*#?\s*ClientAliveCountMax\s+/d' "$f"
    done
fi

# Validate configuration
if sshd -t; then
    chmod 600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
else
    echo "sshd config validation failed; restoring backup" >&2
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    exit 1
fi
```

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PARAM="X11UseLocalhost"
VALUE="yes"

if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: $CONFIG_FILE not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remove any existing X11UseLocalhost lines (commented or not) not inside Match blocks
# We'll rewrite the file, removing existing occurrences before any Match block, then append the correct setting

TMPFILE=$(mktemp)
awk -v param="$PARAM" '
BEGIN { in_match = 0 }
/^[[:space:]]*[Mm]atch[[:space:]]/ { in_match = 1 }
{
    if (!in_match && tolower($0) ~ "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*"tolower(param)"[[:space:]]") {
        next
    }
    print
}
' "$CONFIG_FILE" > "$TMPFILE"

# Insert the directive before the first Match block, or append at end if no Match block
if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*[Mm]atch[[:space:]]' "$TMPFILE"; then
    awk -v param="$PARAM" -v value="$VALUE" '
    BEGIN { inserted = 0 }
    /^[[:space:]]*[Mm]atch[[:space:]]/ && !inserted {
        print param " " value
        inserted = 1
    }
    { print }
    ' "$TMPFILE" > "${TMPFILE}.new"
    mv "${TMPFILE}.new" "$TMPFILE"
else
    # Ensure trailing newline
    [ -s "$TMPFILE" ] && [ "$(tail -c1 "$TMPFILE"; echo x)" != $'\nx' ] && echo "" >> "$TMPFILE"
    echo "${PARAM} ${VALUE}" >> "$TMPFILE"
fi

# Only overwrite if changed
if ! cmp -s "$TMPFILE" "$CONFIG_FILE"; then
    cp --preserve=mode,ownership "$CONFIG_FILE" "${CONFIG_FILE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    cat "$TMPFILE" > "$CONFIG_FILE"
    chmod 0600 "$CONFIG_FILE"
    chown root:root "$CONFIG_FILE"
    echo "Updated $CONFIG_FILE with ${PARAM} ${VALUE}"
    CHANGED=1
else
    echo "$CONFIG_FILE already compliant"
    CHANGED=0
fi

rm -f "$TMPFILE"

# Validate configuration
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "sshd configuration test failed" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Reload sshd if changed and service is active
if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
        systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
        echo "Reloaded sshd service"
    fi
fi

exit 0

```bash
set -euo pipefail
```

PARAM="net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects"
VALUE="0"
CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-icmp-redirects.conf"

# Apply at runtime
sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null
sysctl -w "net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects=${VALUE}" >/dev/null

# Remove conflicting settings from other sysctl config files
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "$CONF_FILE" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.accept_redirects\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "/^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.accept_redirects\s*=/d" "$f"
    fi
    if grep -Eq "^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "/^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=/d" "$f"
    fi
done

# Write persistent configuration
cat > "$CONF_FILE" <<EOF
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = ${VALUE}
net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = ${VALUE}
EOF
chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"

# Reload to ensure the persistent settings take effect
sysctl --system >/dev/null

# Verify
CURRENT=$(sysctl -n "$PARAM")
if [ "$CURRENT" != "$VALUE" ]; then
    echo "Failed to set ${PARAM} to ${VALUE} (current: ${CURRENT})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "ICMP redirect acceptance disabled on all IPv4 interfaces."

mkdir -p /etc/dconf/profile

# Create user profile if it doesn't exist
PROFILE_FILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"
if [ ! -f "$PROFILE_FILE" ] || ! grep -q "^user-db:user" "$PROFILE_FILE" || ! grep -q "^system-db:local" "$PROFILE_FILE"; then
    cat > "$PROFILE_FILE" <<EOF

system-db:local
EOF
fi

# Set screensaver lock enabled
SETTINGS_FILE="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-security-settings"
KEY="lock-enabled"
SECTION="org/gnome/desktop/screensaver"

if [ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]; then
    cat > "$SETTINGS_FILE" <<EOF
[${SECTION}]
${KEY}=true
EOF
else
    if ! grep -q "^\[${SECTION}\]" "$SETTINGS_FILE"; then
        cat >> "$SETTINGS_FILE" <<EOF

[${SECTION}]
${KEY}=true
EOF
    else
        # Section exists; check for key
        if grep -q "^${KEY}=" "$SETTINGS_FILE"; then
            sed -i "s|^${KEY}=.*|${KEY}=true|" "$SETTINGS_FILE"
        else
            sed -i "/^\[${SECTION}\]/a ${KEY}=true" "$SETTINGS_FILE"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Lock the setting so users cannot override
LOCK_FILE="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/00-security-settings-lock"
LOCK_PATH="/${SECTION}/${KEY}"

if [ ! -f "$LOCK_FILE" ] || ! grep -Fxq "$LOCK_PATH" "$LOCK_FILE"; then
    echo "$LOCK_PATH" >> "$LOCK_FILE"
fi

# Update dconf database
dconf update

#!/bin/bash
# Remediation: Ensure rsyslog uses x509/name authentication for remote log forwarding

set -o pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
DIRECTIVE='$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name'

# Remove any incorrect ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode settings from all rsyslog config files
find_files() {
    local files=()
    [ -f "$RSYSLOG_CONF" ] && files+=("$RSYSLOG_CONF")
    if [ -d "$RSYSLOG_DIR" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            files+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$RSYSLOG_DIR" -type f -name "*.conf" -print0)
    fi
    printf '%s\n' "${files[@]}"
}

# Check if directive already correctly set anywhere
found_correct=0
while IFS= read -r file; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$file"; then
        found_correct=1
    fi
done < <(find_files)

# Remove any incorrect entries
while IFS= read -r file; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+' "$file"; then
        if ! grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$file"; then
            sed -i.bak -E '/^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+/d' "$file"
        fi
    fi
done < <(find_files)

# Re-check after cleanup
found_correct=0
while IFS= read -r file; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$file"; then
        found_correct=1
        break
    fi
done < <(find_files)

# If not found, add to main rsyslog.conf
if [ "$found_correct" -eq 0 ]; then
    if [ ! -f "$RSYSLOG_CONF" ]; then
        touch "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
        chmod 0600 "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
    fi
    printf '\n%s\n' "$DIRECTIVE" >> "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
fi

# Restart rsyslog service if active to apply changes
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog || true
fi

# Ensure rsyslog is enabled so setting persists across reboots
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "Remediation complete: rsyslog configured with x509/name peer authentication."
exit 0

```bash
RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
DIRECTIVE='$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name'

# Remove any incorrect ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode settings from all rsyslog config files
find_files() {
    local files=()
    [ -f "$RSYSLOG_CONF" ] && files+=("$RSYSLOG_CONF")
    if [ -d "$RSYSLOG_DIR" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            files+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$RSYSLOG_DIR" -type f -name "*.conf" -print0)
    fi
    printf '%s\n' "${files[@]}"
}

# Check if directive already correctly set anywhere
found_correct=0
while IFS= read -r file; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$file"; then
        found_correct=1
    fi
done < <(find_files)

# Remove any incorrect entries
while IFS= read -r file; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+' "$file"; then
        if ! grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$file"; then
            sed -i.bak -E '/^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+/d' "$file"
        fi
    fi
done < <(find_files)

# Re-check after cleanup
found_correct=0
while IFS= read -r file; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$file"; then
        found_correct=1
        break
    fi
done < <(find_files)

# If not found, add to main rsyslog.conf
if [ "$found_correct" -eq 0 ]; then
    if [ ! -f "$RSYSLOG_CONF" ]; then
        touch "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
        chmod 0600 "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
    fi
    printf '\n%s\n' "$DIRECTIVE" >> "$RSYSLOG_CONF"
fi

# Restart rsyslog service if active to apply changes
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog || true
fi

# Ensure rsyslog is enabled so setting persists across reboots
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "Remediation complete: rsyslog configured with x509/name peer authentication."
exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: audit watches on login/logout and failed authentication records
# Files monitored: /var/log/lastlog, /var/run/faillock (RHEL 8 default)
# Key: logins
#
set -u

FILES=("/var/log/lastlog" "/var/run/faillock")
KEY="logins"

# Ensure audit package present
if ! rpm -q audit >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "audit package not installed; attempting install..."
    dnf install -y audit || yum install -y audit || {
        echo "Failed to install audit" >&2
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Determine rules file location. augenrules uses /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules
RULES_D="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_D}/audit.rules"
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

mkdir -p "${RULES_D}"
[ -f "${RULES_FILE}" ] || touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Function: add a rule to a file if not already present (idempotent)
add_rule_to_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    # Match a line that contains -w <path> and -k <key> for same file/key regardless of perm order
    local path_esc
    path_esc="$(printf '%s\n' "$rule" | grep -oE -- '-w[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]+' | awk '{print $2}')"
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+${path_esc//\//\\/}[[:space:]].*-k[[:space:]]+${KEY}([[:space:]]|$)" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Rule for this file/key exists; make sure it matches desired exactly
        if ! grep -Fxq "$rule" "$file"; then
            # Replace existing line(s) for this file+key with the canonical rule
            local tmp
            tmp="$(mktemp)"
            awk -v path="$path_esc" -v key="$KEY" -v newrule="$rule" '
                {
                    line=$0
                    if (line ~ ("^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+" path "[[:space:]]") && line ~ ("-k[[:space:]]+" key "([[:space:]]|$)")) {
                        if (!printed) { print newrule; printed=1 }
                    } else {
                        print line
                    }
                }
            ' "$file" > "$tmp"
            cat "$tmp" > "$file"
            rm -f "$tmp"
        fi
    else
        printf '%s\n' "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

for f in "${FILES[@]}"; do
    RULE="-w ${f} -p wa -k ${KEY}"
    add_rule_to_file "${RULES_FILE}" "${RULE}"
    # Also ensure it is present in /etc/audit/audit.rules if that file is directly used
    if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES}" ] && [ ! -L "${AUDIT_RULES}" ]; then
        add_rule_to_file "${AUDIT_RULES}" "${RULE}"
    fi
done

# Load rules using augenrules if available (rules.d model), else fallback to auditctl
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
        # Fallback: try to (re)start auditd via service to reload rules
        if command -v service >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi
    }
else
    if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        for f in "${FILES[@]}"; do
            auditctl -w "${f}" -p wa -k "${KEY}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        done
    fi
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded in the running kernel audit subsystem
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    for f in "${FILES[@]}"; do
        if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -E -- "-w[[:space:]]+${f}([[:space:]]|$)" | grep -q "${KEY}"; then
            # Attempt runtime insert
            auditctl -w "${f}" -p wa -k "${KEY}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi
    done
fi

echo "Audit watches for login/logout and failed authentication records configured with key '${KEY}'."
exit 0

for ARCH in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    # Build syscall argument list

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```
# Ensure AIDE rulesets include ACL (acl) verification on RHEL 8

set -e

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

if ! rpm -q aide &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y aide
fi

if [ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file $AIDE_CONF not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup once
if [ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}.bak" ]; then
    cp -a "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Process each line that defines a ruleset (contains '=' with a list of attributes)
# A ruleset line looks like: NAME = attr1+attr2+attr3
# We add 'acl' if missing.
python3 - "$AIDE_CONF" <<'PYEOF'
import re
import sys

path = sys.argv[1]
with open(path, 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Regex for AIDE ruleset definitions: NAME = value(s)
# Ignore lines that are comments, empty, directives (@@), or file selection lines
# (which begin with '/', '!', or '=').
ruleset_re = re.compile(r'^(\s*)([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)(\s*)=(\s*)(.+?)(\s*)$')

new_lines = []
for line in lines:
    stripped = line.lstrip()
    if (not stripped or
        stripped.startswith('#') or
        stripped.startswith('@@') or
        stripped.startswith('/') or
        stripped.startswith('!') or
        stripped.startswith('=')):
        new_lines.append(line)
        continue

    m = ruleset_re.match(line.rstrip('\n'))
    if not m:
        new_lines.append(line)
        continue

    indent, name, sp1, sp2, value, sp3 = m.groups()
    # Split value on '+' to see tokens
    tokens = [t.strip() for t in value.split('+')]
    if 'acl' in tokens:
        new_lines.append(line)
        continue
    # Append acl
    new_value = value.rstrip() + '+acl'
    new_line = f"{indent}{name}{sp1}={sp2}{new_value}\n"
    new_lines.append(new_line)

with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(new_lines)
PYEOF

echo "AIDE configuration updated to include acl in all rulesets."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce a minimum password age of 1 day for all local user
# accounts that currently allow more frequent password changes.
#
# Idempotent: only modifies accounts whose current MIN days < 1.

set -u
set -o pipefail

MIN_DAYS=1
PASSWD_FILE="/etc/passwd"
SHADOW_FILE="/etc/shadow"
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Also enforce a system-wide default for future accounts via /etc/login.defs.
if [[ -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}" ]]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*PASS_MIN_DAYS\s+' "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
        current_default=$(awk '/^\s*PASS_MIN_DAYS\s+/ {print $2; exit}' "${LOGIN_DEFS}")
        if [[ -z "${current_default}" || "${current_default}" -lt "${MIN_DAYS}" ]]; then
            sed -i -E "s/^\s*PASS_MIN_DAYS\s+.*/PASS_MIN_DAYS   ${MIN_DAYS}/" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
            echo "Updated PASS_MIN_DAYS to ${MIN_DAYS} in ${LOGIN_DEFS}."
        fi
    else
        echo "PASS_MIN_DAYS   ${MIN_DAYS}" >> "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
        echo "Added PASS_MIN_DAYS ${MIN_DAYS} to ${LOGIN_DEFS}."
    fi
fi

# Iterate over all local users defined in /etc/shadow.
# Field layout of /etc/shadow:
#   1: username
#   2: password hash
#   3: last change
#   4: minimum age (days)   <-- what we enforce
#   5: maximum age
#   ...
changed=0
checked=0

while IFS=: read -r user pw lastchg mindays rest; do
    [[ -z "${user}" ]] && continue

    # Skip accounts that do not exist in /etc/passwd (stale entries).
    if ! getent passwd "${user}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        continue
    fi

    # Skip locked / no-password / system disabled accounts:
    # entries with '!', '!!', '*', or empty password field are not
    # interactive login accounts; still, per policy, min age applies to
    # all local accounts so we set it anyway if numeric mindays is low.
    checked=$((checked + 1))

    # Default missing/blank mindays to 0.
    if [[ -z "${mindays}" || ! "${mindays}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        mindays=0
    fi

    if (( mindays < MIN_DAYS )); then
        if chage -m "${MIN_DAYS}" "${user}"; then
            echo "Set minimum password age to ${MIN_DAYS} day(s) for user: ${user} (was ${mindays})."
            changed=$((changed + 1))
        else
            echo "WARNING: Failed to set minimum password age for user: ${user}" >&2
        fi
    fi
done < "${SHADOW_FILE}"

echo "Remediation complete. Accounts inspected: ${checked}, accounts modified: ${changed}."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Remediate nullok in PAM configuration on RHEL 8
# Use authselect if in use, otherwise edit files directly

if authselect current &>/dev/null; then
    # authselect is in use
    profile=$(authselect current | awk 'NR==1 {print $3}')
    
    # Get current features
    current_features=$(authselect current | awk 'NR>2 {print $2}' | tr '\n' ' ')
    
    # Check for and remove any nullok related features (there shouldn't be any by default)
    # The main fix is to ensure the PAM files don't contain nullok
    
    # Get profile directory
    profile_dir="/etc/authselect/custom/${profile#custom/}"
    if [[ "$profile" != custom/* ]]; then
        profile_dir="/usr/share/authselect/default/$profile"
    fi
    
    # Remove nullok from authselect profile templates if custom
    if [[ "$profile" == custom/* ]] && [[ -d "$profile_dir" ]]; then
        for f in "$profile_dir"/system-auth "$profile_dir"/password-auth; do
            if [[ -f "$f" ]] && grep -q 'nullok' "$f"; then
                sed -i 's/\s*nullok//g' "$f"
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    # Apply authselect (regenerates /etc/pam.d files from templates)
    authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || authselect select "$profile" $current_features --force
fi

# Also directly ensure the PAM files have no nullok (belt and suspenders, and handles non-authselect case)
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [[ -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
        if grep -q 'nullok' "$pam_file"; then
            sed -i 's/\s*nullok//g' "$pam_file"
        fi
    fi
done

echo "Remediation complete: nullok removed from PAM authentication configuration."

#
# Remediation: Configure rsyslog to use GnuTLS (TLS) as the default network
# stream driver for encrypted log forwarding to remote hosts.
#

set -o pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
DRIVER_DIRECTIVE='$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls'

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "rsyslog package is not installed; installing..."
    yum install -y rsyslog || dnf install -y rsyslog
fi

# Ensure rsyslog-gnutls is installed (provides gtls driver)
if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "rsyslog-gnutls package is not installed; installing..."
    yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls || dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

# Ensure config directory exists
[ -d "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR" ] || mkdir -p "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"

# Remove any conflicting DefaultNetstreamDriver entries (non-gtls) from rsyslog.conf and drop-in files
remove_conflicting() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Remove lines that set DefaultNetstreamDriver to something other than gtls
        sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+(?!gtls)/Id' "$file" 2>/dev/null || \
        sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*\\\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+/Id" "$file"
    fi
}

# Sed doesn't support PCRE lookaheads portably; do it in two passes:
# 1) find files containing DefaultNetstreamDriver
# 2) if the value isn't gtls, remove those lines
for f in "$RSYSLOG_CONF" "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    if grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+' "$f"; then
        # If any line sets it to something other than gtls, remove all such lines
        if grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+' "$f" && \
           ! grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+gtls[[:space:]]*$' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*\\\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+/Id" "$f"
        fi
    fi
done

# Now ensure gtls is set exactly once in a dedicated drop-in file
TLS_CONF="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/tls.conf"

if [ -f "$TLS_CONF" ]; then
    if grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+gtls[[:space:]]*$' "$TLS_CONF"; then
        :
    else
        # Remove any old DefaultNetstreamDriver lines and append gtls
        sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*\\\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+/Id" "$TLS_CONF"
        echo "$DRIVER_DIRECTIVE" >> "$TLS_CONF"
    fi
else
    cat > "$TLS_CONF" <<EOF
# Enforce TLS (GnuTLS) as the default network stream driver for rsyslog
$DRIVER_DIRECTIVE
EOF
fi

chown root:root "$TLS_CONF"
chmod 0644 "$TLS_CONF"

# Verify configuration presence
if ! grep -rEhiq '^[[:space:]]*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver[[:space:]]+gtls[[:space:]]*$' "$RSYSLOG_CONF" "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"/*.conf 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to set \$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Enable and restart rsyslog to persist across reboots
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
systemctl restart rsyslog || {
    echo "ERROR: Failed to restart rsyslog" >&2
    exit 1
}

echo "rsyslog default network stream driver is set to gtls (TLS)."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

UNIT="systemd-coredump.socket"
SERVICE="systemd-coredump.service"

# Stop the socket and service if running
for u in "$UNIT" "$SERVICE"; do
    if systemctl is-active --quiet "$u"; then
        systemctl stop "$u" || true
    fi
done

# Disable and mask the socket to prevent activation
for u in "$UNIT" "$SERVICE"; do
    systemctl disable "$u" 2>/dev/null || true
    if ! systemctl is-enabled "$u" 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^masked$'; then
        systemctl mask "$u" || true
    fi
done

# Ensure sysctl setting to disable core dump pattern handoff to systemd-coredump
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/50-coredump.conf"
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d
cat > "$SYSCTL_FILE" <<'EOF'
kernel.core_pattern=|/bin/false
EOF
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Configure systemd coredump handling to disable storage and processing
COREDUMP_CONF_DIR="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
mkdir -p "$COREDUMP_CONF_DIR"
cat > "${COREDUMP_CONF_DIR}/disable.conf" <<'EOF'
[Coredump]
Storage=none
ProcessSizeMax=0
EOF

# Set limits to prevent core dump creation
LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredump.conf"
mkdir -p /etc/security/limits.d
cat > "$LIMITS_FILE" <<'EOF'
* hard core 0
* soft core 0
EOF

# Reload systemd to pick up changes
systemctl daemon-reload || true

echo "Core dump handling has been disabled."

```bash
done

# Disable and mask the socket to prevent activation
for u in "$UNIT" "$SERVICE"; do
    systemctl disable "$u" 2>/dev/null || true
    if ! systemctl is-enabled "$u" 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^masked$'; then
        systemctl mask "$u" || true
    fi
done

# Ensure sysctl setting to disable core dump pattern handoff to systemd-coredump
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/50-coredump.conf"
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d
cat > "$SYSCTL_FILE" <<'EOF'
kernel.core_pattern=|/bin/false
EOF
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Configure systemd coredump handling to disable storage and processing
COREDUMP_CONF_DIR="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
mkdir -p "$COREDUMP_CONF_DIR"
cat > "${COREDUMP_CONF_DIR}/disable.conf" <<'EOF'
[Coredump]
Storage=none
ProcessSizeMax=0
EOF

# Set limits to prevent core dump creation
LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredump.conf"
mkdir -p /etc/security/limits.d
cat > "$LIMITS_FILE" <<'EOF'
* hard core 0
* soft core 0
EOF

# Reload systemd to pick up changes
systemctl daemon-reload || true

echo "Core dump handling has been disabled."
```

#!/bin/bash
# Remediation: Set default Bash umask to 027 system-wide on RHEL 8

set -euo pipefail

FILES=(/etc/bashrc /etc/profile)

# Include files under /etc/profile.d that may set umask
while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
    FILES+=("$f")
done < <(find /etc/profile.d -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -name "*.sh" \) -print0 2>/dev/null || true)

for f in "${FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue

    # Replace any existing umask lines with umask 027 (preserve leading whitespace)
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+' "$f"; then
        sed -ri 's|^([[:space:]]*)umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+.*$|\1umask 027|g' "$f"
    fi
done

# Ensure /etc/bashrc and /etc/profile have at least one umask 027 entry
for f in /etc/bashrc /etc/profile; do
    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
        if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+027[[:space:]]*$' "$f"; then
            printf '\n# Set default umask for file creation (security hardening)\numask 027\n' >> "$f"
        fi
    fi
done

# Create a dedicated profile.d drop-in to ensure persistence for new sessions
DROPIN="/etc/profile.d/cis_umask.sh"
DESIRED_CONTENT='# Set default umask to 027 for security compliance
umask 027'

if [ ! -f "$DROPIN" ] || [ "$(cat "$DROPIN")" != "$DESIRED_CONTENT" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$DESIRED_CONTENT" > "$DROPIN"
    chmod 0644 "$DROPIN"
    chown root:root "$DROPIN"
fi

echo "Umask 027 has been enforced system-wide."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Configure chrony to operate in client-only mode (no listening port).
# Ensures 'port 0' and 'cmdport 0' are set in /etc/chrony.conf (idempotent).
#

set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"

# If chrony isn't installed, nothing to do.
if ! rpm -q chrony >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "chrony package is not installed; nothing to remediate."
    exit 0
fi

# Create the config file if it doesn't exist (unlikely on RHEL 8 with chrony installed).
if [ ! -f "$CHRONY_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$CHRONY_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$CHRONY_CONF"
    chown root:root "$CHRONY_CONF"
fi

# Backup once.
if [ ! -f "${CHRONY_CONF}.bak.cis" ]; then
    cp -p "$CHRONY_CONF" "${CHRONY_CONF}.bak.cis"
fi

set_directive() {
    local directive="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    # Remove any existing (uncommented) occurrences of the directive.
    # Match lines starting with optional whitespace then the directive followed by whitespace.
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${directive}([[:space:]]+|$)" "$file"; then
        # Replace the first uncommented occurrence with the desired value, delete the rest.
        awk -v d="$directive" -v v="$value" '
            BEGIN { replaced = 0 }
            {
                if ($0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*" d "([[:space:]]+|$)") {
                    if (replaced == 0) {
                        print d " " v
                        replaced = 1
                    }
                    # skip additional occurrences
                    next
                }
                print
            }
            END {
                if (replaced == 0) {
                    print d " " v
                }
            }
        ' "$file" > "${file}.tmp.$$"
        mv "${file}.tmp.$$" "$file"
    else
        # Directive not present at all; append it.
        printf '%s %s\n' "$directive" "$value" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Enforce client-only mode:
#   port 0     -> disables NTP server socket (no listening on UDP/123 for serving time)
#   cmdport 0  -> disables the chronyc command/monitoring socket on the network
set_directive "port"    "0" "$CHRONY_CONF"
set_directive "cmdport" "0" "$CHRONY_CONF"

# Ensure secure permissions on the config.
chown root:root "$CHRONY_CONF"
chmod 0644 "$CHRONY_CONF"

# Enable and restart chronyd so changes persist and take effect now.
systemctl enable chronyd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd; then
    systemctl restart chronyd
else
    systemctl start chronyd || true
fi

echo "chrony has been configured for client-only operation (port 0, cmdport 0)."

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
```

SYSCTL_PARAM="kernel.randomize_va_space"
SYSCTL_VALUE="2"
CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-kernel_randomize_va_space.conf"

# Remove any conflicting settings in other sysctl config files
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "$CONF_FILE" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=/d" "$f"
    fi
done

# Write the desired setting to our managed conf file
if [ -f "$CONF_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "$CONF_FILE"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*|${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}|" "$CONF_FILE"
    else
        printf '%s = %s\n' "$SYSCTL_PARAM" "$SYSCTL_VALUE" >> "$CONF_FILE"
    fi
else
    printf '%s = %s\n' "$SYSCTL_PARAM" "$SYSCTL_VALUE" > "$CONF_FILE"
fi

chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"

# Apply at runtime
sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}"
sysctl --system >/dev/null

# Verify
current="$(sysctl -n "$SYSCTL_PARAM")"
if [ "$current" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is '${current}', expected '${SYSCTL_VALUE}'" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "ASLR configured: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}"

#!/bin/bash

# Determine which rules file to use based on augenrules vs auditctl
if grep -q "^\s*ExecStartPost.*augenrules" /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service 2>/dev/null || \
   [ -f /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules ]; then
    USE_AUGENRULES=1
else
    USE_AUGENRULES=0
fi

# Function to add/update rules in a file
update_rules_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local arch="$2"
    local rule="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S removexattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
    local rule_root="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S removexattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"

    # Remove existing removexattr lines for this arch (to normalize)
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Use a temp file
        grep -v -E "^\s*-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+-F\s+arch=${arch}\s+.*-S\s+[^ ]*removexattr" "$file" > "${file}.tmp" || true
        mv "${file}.tmp" "$file"
    fi

    echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    echo "$rule_root" >> "$file"
}

if [ "$USE_AUGENRULES" -eq 1 ]; then
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
    mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d
    touch "$RULES_FILE"

    # Also clean any removexattr rules from other files in rules.d
    for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
        [ "$f" = "$RULES_FILE" ] && continue
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -qE "^\s*-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+.*-S\s+[^ ]*removexattr" "$f"; then
            grep -v -E "^\s*-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+.*-S\s+[^ ]*removexattr" "$f" > "${f}.tmp"
            mv "${f}.tmp" "$f"
        fi
    done

    update_rules_file "$RULES_FILE" "b32"
    update_rules_file "$RULES_FILE" "b64"

    augenrules --load
else
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
    touch "$RULES_FILE"
    update_rules_file "$RULES_FILE" "b32"
    update_rules_file "$RULES_FILE" "b64"

    auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Ensure auditd will reload
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl kill --kill-who=main --signal=SIGHUP auditd 2>/dev/null || service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

set -o pipefail

UNLOCK_TIME=900
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure faillock.conf exists
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chown root:root "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Helper: set a key = value line in faillock.conf idempotently
set_faillock_option() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$FAILLOCK_CONF"

    if grep -Eq "^\s*#?\s*${key}\s*=" "$file"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*#?\s*(${key})\s*=.*|\1 = ${value}|" "$file"
    else
        echo "${key} = ${value}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Enforce lockout policy values
set_faillock_option "deny"         "3"
set_faillock_option "unlock_time"  "${UNLOCK_TIME}"
set_faillock_option "fail_interval" "900"

# Validate that unlock_time meets policy (>=900 and not 0)
CURRENT_UNLOCK=$(grep -E "^\s*unlock_time\s*=" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" | tail -n1 | awk -F= '{gsub(/ /,"",$2); print $2}')
if [ -z "$CURRENT_UNLOCK" ] || [ "$CURRENT_UNLOCK" -lt 900 ]; then
    set_faillock_option "unlock_time" "${UNLOCK_TIME}"
fi

# Ensure authselect is used and integrate faillock into the PAM stack
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Determine current profile
    CURRENT_PROFILE="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk -F': ' '/Profile ID/ {print $2}')"

    if [ -z "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]; then
        # No profile selected; select a sane default with faillock enabled
        authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
    else
        # Enable with-faillock feature idempotently
        if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        fi
    fi

    # Apply changes
    authselect apply-changes -b >/dev/null 2>&1 || authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
    # Fallback: directly edit PAM files if authselect not present
    for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
        [ -f "$pam_file" ] || continue

        if ! grep -q "pam_faillock.so preauth" "$pam_file"; then
            sed -ri '/^auth\s+required\s+pam_env.so/a auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth silent' "$pam_file"
        fi
        if ! grep -q "pam_faillock.so authfail" "$pam_file"; then
            sed -ri '/^auth\s+sufficient\s+pam_unix.so/a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail' "$pam_file"
        fi
        if ! grep -q "account\s\+required\s\+pam_faillock.so" "$pam_file"; then
            sed -ri '/^account\s+required\s+pam_unix.so/i account     required      pam_faillock.so' "$pam_file"
        fi
    done
fi

echo "Account lockout policy configured: deny=3, unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}, fail_interval=900"
exit 0

)

    local rule="$2"

    local exitcode

    exitcode=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oP '(?<=-F exit=)\S+')

       grep -q "\-S\s\+${syscall}" 2>/dev/null; then
        :

           echo "$line" | grep -q "key=access\|-k access"; then
            found=1
            break
        fi
    done < <(grep -E "^\s*-a\s+always,exit" "$file" 2>/dev/null)

    if [ "$found" -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Ensure files exist

touch "$AUDIT_MAIN_FILE"

done

    add_rule_if_missing "$AUDIT_MAIN_FILE" "$rule"

    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    else
        auditctl -R "$AUDIT_MAIN_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
CUSTOM_CONF="${RSYSLOG_DIR}/50-remote-access.conf"

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y rsyslog
fi

# Ensure rsyslog.d directory exists
mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_DIR}"

# Define required rules
declare -A RULES=(
    ["auth.*"]="/var/log/secure"
    ["authpriv.*"]="/var/log/secure"
    ["daemon.*"]="/var/log/messages"
)

# Function: check if a rule already exists in any rsyslog config
rule_exists() {
    local facility="$1"
    local target="$2"
    # Escape dots for regex
    local fac_escaped
    fac_escaped=$(printf '%s\n' "$facility" | sed 's/[.[\*^$/]/\\&/g')
    local tgt_escaped
    tgt_escaped=$(printf '%s\n' "$target" | sed 's/[.[\*^$/]/\\&/g')

    # Search in main rsyslog.conf and all files in /etc/rsyslog.d/
    local files=("${RSYSLOG_CONF}")
    if [ -d "${RSYSLOG_DIR}" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            files+=("$f")
        done < <(find "${RSYSLOG_DIR}" -type f -name "*.conf" -print0)
    fi

    for f in "${files[@]}"; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        # Look for a non-commented line containing the facility and target
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${fac_escaped}[[:space:]]+-?${tgt_escaped}([[:space:]]|$)" "$f"; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# Ensure custom conf file exists
if [ ! -f "${CUSTOM_CONF}" ]; then
    cat > "${CUSTOM_CONF}" <<'EOF'
# Remote access and authentication logging - managed by remediation script
EOF
    chmod 0600 "${CUSTOM_CONF}"
    chown root:root "${CUSTOM_CONF}"
fi

# Append missing rules
CHANGED=0
for facility in "${!RULES[@]}"; do
    target="${RULES[$facility]}"
    if ! rule_exists "$facility" "$target"; then
        printf '%s    %s\n' "$facility" "$target" >> "${CUSTOM_CONF}"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

# Ensure target log files exist with proper perms
for logfile in /var/log/secure /var/log/messages; do
    if [ ! -e "$logfile" ]; then
        touch "$logfile"
    fi
    chown root:root "$logfile"
    chmod 0600 "$logfile"
done

# Ensure permissions on our conf file
chmod 0600 "${CUSTOM_CONF}"
chown root:root "${CUSTOM_CONF}"

# Enable and (re)start rsyslog
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ] || ! systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog
fi

exit 0

    current_target=$(systemctl get-default 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        systemctl set-default multi-user.target
    fi
fi

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

# Remove X Windows package group and xorg-x11-server-common if present
if rpm -q xorg-x11-server-common &>/dev/null; then
    dnf remove -y xorg-x11-server-\* || true
fi

# Ensure any remaining xorg-x11-server packages are removed
mapfile -t xpkgs < <(rpm -qa 'xorg-x11-server*' 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ "${#xpkgs[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    dnf remove -y "${xpkgs[@]}" || true
fi

# Set default systemd target to multi-user (non-graphical) to prevent graphical boot
if command -v systemctl &>/dev/null; then
    current_target=$(systemctl get-default 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        systemctl set-default multi-user.target
    fi
fi

exit 0
```

EOF

CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"

if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*dictcheck\s*=' "$CONF"; then
    sed -i -E 's|^\s*#?\s*dictcheck\s*=.*|dictcheck = 1|' "$CONF"
else
    echo "dictcheck = 1" >> "$CONF"
fi

# Also check drop-in config directory if it exists
if [ -d /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eq '^\s*dictcheck\s*=\s*0' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E 's|^\s*dictcheck\s*=.*|dictcheck = 1|' "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Ensure PAM stack uses pam_pwquality (system-auth and password-auth)
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    [ -f "$pam_file" ] || continue

    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*password\s+.*\s+pam_pwquality\.so' "$pam_file"; then
        # Insert pam_pwquality line before pam_unix in password stack
        if grep -Eq '^\s*password\s+.*\s+pam_unix\.so' "$pam_file"; then

        fi
    fi
done

# If authselect is in use, apply changes via authselect
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
fi

echo "Dictionary word checking (dictcheck=1) has been enforced in $CONF."

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
```
#
# Remediation: Configure GNOME screensaver lock-delay to 0 seconds system-wide.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-security-settings"
LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-security-settings-lock"

# Check if GNOME / dconf is installed; if not, nothing to do.
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "dconf not found. GNOME desktop does not appear to be installed. Nothing to remediate."
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure the user profile exists and references the 'local' database.
mkdir -p "$(dirname "${DCONF_PROFILE}")"
if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE}" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
else
    grep -qxF 'user-db:user'     "${DCONF_PROFILE}" || echo 'user-db:user'     >> "${DCONF_PROFILE}"
    grep -qxF 'system-db:local'  "${DCONF_PROFILE}" || echo 'system-db:local'  >> "${DCONF_PROFILE}"
fi

# Ensure directories exist.
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# Create/update the settings file with lock-delay=0.
if [ -f "${SETTINGS_FILE}" ]; then
    if grep -q '^\[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver\]' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        # Section exists; make sure lock-delay is set to 0.
        if grep -q '^lock-delay=' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
            sed -i 's/^lock-delay=.*/lock-delay=uint32 0/' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
        else
            # Insert the key right after the section header.
            sed -i '/^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/screensaver\]/a lock-delay=uint32 0' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
        fi
    else
        # Append the section and key.
        printf '\n[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]\nlock-delay=uint32 0\n' >> "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    cat > "${SETTINGS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0
EOF
fi

# Lock the setting so users cannot override it.
if [ -f "${LOCKS_FILE}" ]; then
    grep -qxF '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay' "${LOCKS_FILE}" \
        || echo '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay' >> "${LOCKS_FILE}"
else
    echo '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay' > "${LOCKS_FILE}"
fi

# Enforce sane permissions.
chmod 0644 "${DCONF_PROFILE}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}" "${LOCKS_FILE}"

# Update the dconf database so changes take effect.
dconf update

echo "GNOME screensaver lock-delay has been configured to 0 seconds system-wide."

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
```

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/00-disable-root-login.conf"

# Ensure the main sshd_config exists
if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SSHD_CONFIG} does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Update main sshd_config: ensure PermitRootLogin no
if grep -qiE '^\s*PermitRootLogin\s+' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    sed -ri 's/^\s*PermitRootLogin\s+.*/PermitRootLogin no/I' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
else
    printf '\nPermitRootLogin no\n' >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
fi

# Create/refresh drop-in file to guarantee precedence
mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"
chmod 0755 "${DROPIN_DIR}"

cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Managed file: disallow direct remote root login via SSH
PermitRootLogin no
EOF
chmod 0600 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Remove any conflicting PermitRootLogin directives in other drop-ins
if [ -d "${DROPIN_DIR}" ]; then
    for f in "${DROPIN_DIR}"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        [ "$f" = "${DROPIN_FILE}" ] && continue
        if grep -qiE '^\s*PermitRootLogin\s+' "$f"; then
            sed -ri 's/^\s*PermitRootLogin\s+.*/PermitRootLogin no/I' "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Validate configuration
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sshd -t
fi

# Reload sshd to apply changes if running
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
fi

# Ensure sshd is enabled to persist across reboots
systemctl enable sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "PermitRootLogin has been set to 'no'."

```bash
    fi
```

# Also add to /etc/audit/audit.rules if it exists (loaded rules file)
if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]; then

            echo "$RULE" >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
        fi
    done
fi

# Reload audit rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

if systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    service auditd restart || systemctl restart auditd || true
fi

exit 0

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```
#
# Remediation: Ensure per-user shell initialization files are no more
# permissive than 0740 (u=rwx,g=r,o=). Does not loosen stricter perms.
#

set -u

# Initialization dot-files commonly used by shells
INIT_FILES=(
    ".bashrc"
    ".bash_profile"
    ".bash_login"
    ".profile"
    ".bash_logout"
    ".cshrc"
    ".tcshrc"
    ".login"
    ".logout"
    ".kshrc"
    ".zshrc"
    ".zprofile"
    ".zlogin"
    ".zlogout"
    ".zshenv"
    ".inputrc"
    ".bash_aliases"
    ".bash_functions"
    ".bashrc.local"
)

# Maximum allowed mode
MAX_MODE=0740

tighten_file() {
    local f="$1"
    [ -e "$f" ] || return 0
    [ -L "$f" ] && return 0
    [ -f "$f" ] || return 0

    local cur_mode
    cur_mode=$(stat -c "%a" "$f" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
    # normalize to 4 digits
    cur_mode=$(printf "%04o" "0$cur_mode")

    # Compute new mode = cur_mode AND MAX_MODE (bitwise)
    local new_mode
    new_mode=$(printf "%04o" $(( 0$cur_mode & 0$MAX_MODE )))

    if [ "$cur_mode" != "$new_mode" ]; then
        chmod "$new_mode" "$f" && \
            echo "Adjusted permissions on $f: $cur_mode -> $new_mode"
    fi
}

process_home() {
    local home="$1"
    [ -d "$home" ] || return 0
    local f
    for f in "${INIT_FILES[@]}"; do
        tighten_file "$home/$f"
    done
}

# Process root account
ROOT_HOME=$(getent passwd root | awk -F: '{print $6}')
[ -n "$ROOT_HOME" ] && process_home "$ROOT_HOME"

# Determine UID_MIN / UID_MAX for human users
UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN\s+/ {print $2; exit}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)
UID_MAX=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MAX\s+/ {print $2; exit}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)
: "${UID_MIN:=1000}"
: "${UID_MAX:=60000}"

# Process each human user
while IFS=: read -r user _ uid _ _ home shell; do
    [ "$uid" -ge "$UID_MIN" ] || continue
    [ "$uid" -le "$UID_MAX" ] || continue
    # Skip nologin/false shells for non-interactive accounts
    case "$shell" in
        */nologin|*/false|"") continue ;;
    esac
    process_home "$home"
done < <(getent passwd)

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot reaction on RHEL 8 (idempotent).
#
set -euo pipefail

TARGET="/etc/systemd/system/ctrl-alt-del.target"

# Mask the ctrl-alt-del.target so systemd ignores the key sequence.
# Masking creates a symlink to /dev/null in /etc/systemd/system, which
# overrides the unit shipped in /usr/lib/systemd/system and therefore
# survives package updates.

if [ -L "${TARGET}" ] && [ "$(readlink -f "${TARGET}")" = "/dev/null" ]; then
    echo "ctrl-alt-del.target is already masked; no action required."
else
    # Remove any pre-existing file/link that is not the desired mask.
    if [ -e "${TARGET}" ] || [ -L "${TARGET}" ]; then
        rm -f "${TARGET}"
    fi
    systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target
    echo "ctrl-alt-del.target has been masked."
fi

# Reload systemd so the change takes effect immediately.
systemctl daemon-reload

# Verify.
if systemctl is-enabled ctrl-alt-del.target 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^masked$'; then
    echo "Verification: ctrl-alt-del.target is masked."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to mask ctrl-alt-del.target." >&2
    exit 1
fi

BOOT_SRC="$(findmnt -k -n -o SOURCE "${MOUNT_POINT}" 2>/dev/null)"
ROOT_SRC="$(findmnt -k -n -o SOURCE / 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -z "${BOOT_SRC}" ] || [ "${BOOT_SRC}" = "${ROOT_SRC}" ]; then
    echo "Notice: ${MOUNT_POINT} is not on a separate filesystem. Nothing to do."
    exit 0
fi

echo "Processing ${MOUNT_POINT} (${BOOT_SRC})..."

# --- Update /etc/fstab to persist across reboots ---
if [ ! -f "${FSTAB}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${FSTAB} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup fstab once
if [ ! -f "${FSTAB}.bak.nosuid" ]; then
    cp -a "${FSTAB}" "${FSTAB}.bak.nosuid"
fi

# Locate the /boot line in fstab (skip comments/blank lines) and update options
tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
updated=0
found=0
while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "${line}" ]; do
    # Preserve comments and empty lines
    if [[ -z "${line}" || "${line}" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${tmpfile}"
        continue
    fi

    # Split fields
    read -r f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6 <<< "${line}"

    if [ "${f2}" = "${MOUNT_POINT}" ]; then
        found=1
        opts="${f4}"

        # Remove any existing suid/nosuid tokens, then add nosuid
        # Split opts by comma, filter, rejoin
        IFS=',' read -r -a opt_arr <<< "${opts}"
        new_opts=()
        for o in "${opt_arr[@]}"; do
            case "${o}" in
                suid|nosuid) ;; # drop
                "") ;;          # drop empties
                *) new_opts+=("${o}") ;;
            esac
        done
        new_opts+=("nosuid")
        new_opts_str="$(IFS=','; echo "${new_opts[*]}")"

        if [ "${opts}" != "${new_opts_str}" ]; then
            updated=1
        fi

        printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "${f1}" "${f2}" "${f3}" "${new_opts_str}" "${f5:-0}" "${f6:-0}" >> "${tmpfile}"
    else
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${tmpfile}"
    fi
done < "${FSTAB}"

if [ "${found}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Warning: No entry for ${MOUNT_POINT} found in ${FSTAB}; skipping fstab update."
    rm -f "${tmpfile}"
else
    if [ "${updated}" -eq 1 ]; then
        cat "${tmpfile}" > "${FSTAB}"
        echo "Updated ${FSTAB}: ${MOUNT_POINT} now includes 'nosuid'."
    else
        echo "${FSTAB} already contains 'nosuid' for ${MOUNT_POINT}."
    fi
    rm -f "${tmpfile}"
fi

# --- Apply setting to the running system ---
CURRENT_OPTS="$(findmnt -k -n -o OPTIONS "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
if echo ",${CURRENT_OPTS}," | grep -q ",nosuid,"; then
    echo "Runtime: ${MOUNT_POINT} is already mounted with nosuid."
else
    echo "Remounting ${MOUNT_POINT} with nosuid..."
    if mount -o remount,nosuid "${MOUNT_POINT}"; then
        echo "Successfully remounted ${MOUNT_POINT} with nosuid."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to remount ${MOUNT_POINT} with nosuid." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Final verification
FINAL_OPTS="$(findmnt -k -n -o OPTIONS "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
if echo ",${FINAL_OPTS}," | grep -q ",nosuid,"; then
    echo "Verification passed: ${MOUNT_POINT} options: ${FINAL_OPTS}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Verification failed. ${MOUNT_POINT} options: ${FINAL_OPTS}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Get UID_MIN from /etc/login.defs
UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs)
[ -z "$UID_MIN" ] && UID_MIN=1000

# Syscalls to monitor for DAC permission changes
SYSCALLS="chmod fchmod fchmodat"

KEY="perm_mod"
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    yum install -y audit
fi

# Function to add rule if not present
add_rule() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Create rules file if it doesn't exist
touch "$RULES_FILE"

# Remove any old/conflicting perm_mod rules from all rule files to ensure idempotency
for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    sed -i '/-F key=perm_mod/d;/-k perm_mod/d' "$f"
done

# Build syscall list argument
SYSCALL_ARGS=""
for sc in $SYSCALLS; do
    SYSCALL_ARGS="$SYSCALL_ARGS -S $sc"
done

# Add rules for both 64-bit and 32-bit
RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64${SYSCALL_ARGS} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32${SYSCALL_ARGS} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"

add_rule "$RULE_64" "$RULES_FILE"
add_rule "$RULE_32" "$RULES_FILE"

# Also update /etc/audit/audit.rules to ensure runtime consistency
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES" ]; then
    sed -i '/-F key=perm_mod/d;/-k perm_mod/d' "$AUDIT_RULES"
    add_rule "$RULE_64" "$AUDIT_RULES"
    add_rule "$RULE_32" "$AUDIT_RULES"
fi

# Reload audit rules
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || service auditd restart
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disallow SSH logins to accounts with empty passwords.
# Ensures PermitEmptyPasswords is set to "no" in the SSH daemon configuration.
#

set -o nounset
set -o pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_D="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG_D}/00-disable-empty-passwords.conf"
PARAM="PermitEmptyPasswords"
VALUE="no"

# Ensure the main config file exists
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SSHD_CONFIG} does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup once
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak"
fi

# Function to update a config file idempotently
update_config() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Replace existing (possibly commented-out with leading spaces handled) lines
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+.*|${PARAM} ${VALUE}|I" "${file}"
    elif grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+.*|${PARAM} ${VALUE}|I" "${file}"
    else
        # Append if it doesn't exist at all
        printf '\n%s %s\n' "${PARAM}" "${VALUE}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# On RHEL 8, /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf files are Included and can override.
# Sanitize any drop-in files that might override with a permissive value.
if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_D}" ]]; then
    shopt -s nullglob
    for f in "${SSHD_CONFIG_D}"/*.conf; do
        # Skip our own file; we'll write it below.
        if [[ "$(readlink -f "${f}")" == "$(readlink -f "${DROPIN_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || echo "${DROPIN_FILE}")" ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        if grep -qEi "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+.*|${PARAM} ${VALUE}|I" "${f}"
        fi
    done
    shopt -u nullglob
fi

# Update the main sshd_config
update_config "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Create/refresh a drop-in file to enforce the setting even if includes are used
mkdir -p "${SSHD_CONFIG_D}"
cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<EOF
# Managed by remediation script - do not edit
# Explicitly forbid SSH logins to accounts with empty passwords
${PARAM} ${VALUE}
EOF

chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chmod 0600 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chmod 0600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null || true

# Validate the sshd configuration before restarting
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd configuration test failed. Reverting drop-in." >&2
        rm -f "${DROPIN_FILE}"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Restart sshd to apply the change (persists across reboots via config file)
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -qE '^sshd\.service'; then
        systemctl restart sshd.service || systemctl reload sshd.service || true
        systemctl enable sshd.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    elif systemctl list-unit-files | grep -qE '^ssh\.service'; then
        systemctl restart ssh.service || systemctl reload ssh.service || true
        systemctl enable ssh.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

echo "SSH ${PARAM} has been set to '${VALUE}'."
exit 0

set -u

RULE='-a always,exit -F path=/usr/sbin/setfiles -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged'

# Locate setfiles binary path (default to /usr/sbin/setfiles)
SETFILES_PATH="/usr/sbin/setfiles"
if [ ! -e "$SETFILES_PATH" ]; then
    if command -v setfiles >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        SETFILES_PATH="$(command -v setfiles)"
        RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${SETFILES_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged"
    fi
fi

update_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local tmp
    # Ensure parent directory exists
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$file")"
    touch "$file"

    # Remove any existing rules referencing setfiles to avoid duplicates/conflicts
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    grep -v -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+path=${SETFILES_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "$file" > "$tmp" || true
    mv "$tmp" "$file"

    # Append the canonical rule
    echo "$RULE" >> "$file"
}

# Handle augenrules-style rules directory
if [ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]; then
    # Clean setfiles-related lines from other rule files in rules.d to avoid duplicates

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -u

RULE='-a always,exit -F path=/usr/sbin/setfiles -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged'

# Locate setfiles binary path (default to /usr/sbin/setfiles)
SETFILES_PATH="/usr/sbin/setfiles"
if [ ! -e "$SETFILES_PATH" ]; then
    if command -v setfiles >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        SETFILES_PATH="$(command -v setfiles)"
        RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${SETFILES_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged"
    fi
fi

update_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local tmp
    # Ensure parent directory exists
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$file")"
    touch "$file"

    # Remove any existing rules referencing setfiles to avoid duplicates/conflicts
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    grep -v -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+path=${SETFILES_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "$file" > "$tmp" || true
    mv "$tmp" "$file"

    # Append the canonical rule
    echo "$RULE" >> "$file"
}

# Handle augenrules-style rules directory
if [ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]; then
    # Clean setfiles-related lines from other rule files in rules.d to avoid duplicates
    for f in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if [ "$f" != "/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules" ]; then
            tmp="$(mktemp)"
            grep -v -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+path=${SETFILES_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "$f" > "$tmp" || true
            mv "$tmp" "$f"
            chmod 0640 "$f" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done
    update_file /etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules
    chmod 0640 /etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Handle single consolidated audit.rules file
if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ] || [ ! -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]; then
    update_file /etc/audit/audit.rules
    chmod 0640 /etc/audit/audit.rules 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Try to reload rules so the rule is effective now
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # If augenrules did not load (or unavailable), attempt direct injection
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+path=${SETFILES_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
        auditctl "$RULE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            auditctl -a always,exit -F path="${SETFILES_PATH}" -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0
```

set -euo pipefail

# Ensure coredump.conf drops core dumps
CONF_DIR="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
CONF_FILE="${CONF_DIR}/disable-coredumps.conf"

mkdir -p "$CONF_DIR"
cat > "$CONF_FILE" <<'EOF'
[Coredump]
Storage=none
ProcessSizeMax=0
EOF
chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"

# Also update main coredump.conf if present
MAIN_CONF="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf"
if [ -f "$MAIN_CONF" ]; then
    if grep -qE '^\s*Storage=' "$MAIN_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's|^\s*Storage=.*|Storage=none|' "$MAIN_CONF"
    else
        if grep -qE '^\s*\[Coredump\]' "$MAIN_CONF"; then
            sed -i '/^\s*\[Coredump\]/a Storage=none' "$MAIN_CONF"
        else
            printf '\n[Coredump]\nStorage=none\n' >> "$MAIN_CONF"
        fi
    fi
    if grep -qE '^\s*ProcessSizeMax=' "$MAIN_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's|^\s*ProcessSizeMax=.*|ProcessSizeMax=0|' "$MAIN_CONF"
    else
        sed -i '/^\s*\[Coredump\]/a ProcessSizeMax=0' "$MAIN_CONF"
    fi
fi

# Set limits.conf hard core 0
LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredumps.conf"
cat > "$LIMITS_FILE" <<'EOF'
* hard core 0
EOF
chmod 0644 "$LIMITS_FILE"

# sysctl: fs.suid_dumpable = 0 and kernel.core_pattern
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-coredumps.conf"
cat > "$SYSCTL_FILE" <<'EOF'
fs.suid_dumpable = 0
kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false
EOF
chmod 0644 "$SYSCTL_FILE"

# Apply sysctl immediately
sysctl -w fs.suid_dumpable=0 >/dev/null
sysctl -w kernel.core_pattern='|/bin/false' >/dev/null

# Mask systemd-coredump socket to prevent handling
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^systemd-coredump.socket'; then
    systemctl disable --now systemd-coredump.socket 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Reload systemd config
systemctl daemon-reload

# Apply current shell ulimit
ulimit -S -c 0 || true
ulimit -H -c 0 || true

echo "Core dump storage has been disabled."

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
```

PARAM="audit_backlog_limit=8192"
GRUB_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

# Update current and future kernels via grubby
if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Remove any existing audit_backlog_limit args, then add the desired one
    current_args=$(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | awk -F\" '/^args=/{print $2}' | tr ' ' '\n' | sort -u)
    for arg in $current_args; do
        case "$arg" in
            audit_backlog_limit=*)
                if [ "$arg" != "$PARAM" ]; then
                    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --remove-args="$arg" || true
                fi
                ;;
        esac
    done
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="$PARAM"
fi

# Ensure persistence across future kernel installs via /etc/default/grub
if [ -f "$GRUB_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_FILE"; then
        # Extract current value
        line=$(grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_FILE")
        value=$(echo "$line" | sed -e 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=//' -e 's/^"//' -e 's/"$//')
        # Remove any existing audit_backlog_limit=
        new_value=$(echo "$value" | sed -E 's/(^|[[:space:]])audit_backlog_limit=[^[:space:]]*//g' | sed -E 's/[[:space:]]+/ /g' | sed -E 's/^ //; s/ $//')
        if [ -n "$new_value" ]; then
            new_value="$new_value $PARAM"
        else
            new_value="$PARAM"
        fi
        sed -i "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${new_value}\"|" "$GRUB_FILE"
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"$PARAM\"" >> "$GRUB_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Regenerate grub configuration
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG=$(find /boot/efi/EFI -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
    [ -z "$GRUB_CFG" ] && GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

if command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "$GRUB_CFG" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Remediation: Enforce a finite sudo timestamp_timeout to require periodic re-authentication.

set -u

SUDOERS_DIR="/etc/sudoers.d"
CONFIG_FILE="${SUDOERS_DIR}/timestamp_timeout"
TIMEOUT_VALUE=5

# Ensure sudoers.d exists
if [ ! -d "${SUDOERS_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${SUDOERS_DIR}"
    chmod 0750 "${SUDOERS_DIR}"
fi

# Remove any existing Defaults timestamp_timeout lines in /etc/sudoers (uncommented)
# that are missing, negative, or otherwise not compliant. We'll replace with our drop-in file.
if [ -f /etc/sudoers ]; then
    # Backup once
    if [ ! -f /etc/sudoers.bak.timestamp_timeout ]; then
        cp -a /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.bak.timestamp_timeout
    fi
    # Remove lines setting timestamp_timeout (any value) from /etc/sudoers
    sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*Defaults[[:space:]]+.*timestamp_timeout[[:space:]]*=/d' /etc/sudoers
fi

# Remove any other drop-in files that set timestamp_timeout to a non-compliant value
if [ -d "${SUDOERS_DIR}" ]; then
    for f in "${SUDOERS_DIR}"/*; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        [ "$f" = "${CONFIG_FILE}" ] && continue
        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*Defaults[[:space:]]+.*timestamp_timeout[[:space:]]*=' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*Defaults[[:space:]]+.*timestamp_timeout[[:space:]]*=/d' "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Write the drop-in file with a finite, non-negative timeout
TMPFILE="$(mktemp)"
cat > "${TMPFILE}" <<EOF
Defaults timestamp_timeout=${TIMEOUT_VALUE}
EOF

# Validate syntax before applying
if visudo -cf "${TMPFILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    install -m 0440 -o root -g root "${TMPFILE}" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    rm -f "${TMPFILE}"
else
    rm -f "${TMPFILE}"
    echo "ERROR: sudoers syntax validation failed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure correct permissions
chmod 0440 "${CONFIG_FILE}"
chown root:root "${CONFIG_FILE}"

# Final validation of the entire sudoers configuration
if ! visudo -c >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: sudoers configuration invalid after remediation." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "sudo timestamp_timeout set to ${TIMEOUT_VALUE} minutes via ${CONFIG_FILE}"
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure every configured upstream time source in chrony has
# an explicit maxpoll value set, so the system clock is polled at a
# bounded maximum interval.
#
# Applies to RHEL 8 with chrony as the time daemon.

set -o pipefail

MAXPOLL_VALUE="${MAXPOLL_VALUE:-10}"
CHRONY_MAIN_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
CHRONY_CONF_D="/etc/chrony.d"

# Collect candidate configuration files.
declare -a CONF_FILES=()
[ -f "$CHRONY_MAIN_CONF" ] && CONF_FILES+=("$CHRONY_MAIN_CONF")
if [ -d "$CHRONY_CONF_D" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        CONF_FILES+=("$f")
    done < <(find "$CHRONY_CONF_D" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

if [ ${#CONF_FILES[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No chrony configuration files found; installing chrony configuration is out of scope." >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

for conf in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -w "$conf" ] || { echo "Cannot write to $conf, skipping." >&2; continue; }

    # Create a working copy.
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    cp -a --preserve=all "$conf" "$tmp"

    # Process each line: if the (non-comment, non-blank) line starts with
    # server, pool, or peer, ensure maxpoll <val> is present. If maxpoll
    # already exists, normalize it to the desired value.
    awk -v mp="$MAXPOLL_VALUE" '
    {
        line = $0
        # Preserve blank/comment lines unchanged.
        stripped = line
        sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, "", stripped)
        if (stripped == "" || substr(stripped,1,1) == "#") {
            print line
            next
        }

        # Split leading whitespace from directive.
        match(line, /^[[:space:]]*/)
        lead = substr(line, 1, RLENGTH)
        rest = substr(line, RLENGTH + 1)

        # First token is the directive.
        n = split(rest, toks, /[[:space:]]+/)
        directive = tolower(toks[1])

        if (directive == "server" || directive == "pool" || directive == "peer") {
            # Separate any trailing inline comment.
            comment = ""
            body = rest
            hashidx = index(rest, "#")
            if (hashidx > 0) {
                body = substr(rest, 1, hashidx - 1)
                comment = substr(rest, hashidx)
                sub(/[[:space:]]+$/, "", body)
            }

            if (match(body, /(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/)) {
                # Replace existing maxpoll value.
                newbody = ""
                remaining = body
                while (match(remaining, /(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/)) {
                    pre = substr(remaining, 1, RSTART - 1)
                    matched = substr(remaining, RSTART, RLENGTH)
                    # Preserve any leading whitespace inside the match.
                    lead_ws = ""
                    if (match(matched, /^[[:space:]]+/)) {
                        lead_ws = substr(matched, 1, RLENGTH)
                    }
                    newbody = newbody pre lead_ws "maxpoll " mp
                    remaining = substr(remaining, RSTART + RLENGTH)
                }
                newbody = newbody remaining
                body = newbody
            } else {
