#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure every configured upstream time source in chrony has
# an explicit maxpoll value set, so the system clock is polled at a
# bounded maximum interval.
#
# Applies to RHEL 8 with chrony as the time daemon.

set -o pipefail

MAXPOLL_VALUE="${MAXPOLL_VALUE:-10}"
CHRONY_MAIN_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
CHRONY_CONF_D="/etc/chrony.d"

# Collect candidate configuration files.
declare -a CONF_FILES=()
[ -f "$CHRONY_MAIN_CONF" ] && CONF_FILES+=("$CHRONY_MAIN_CONF")
if [ -d "$CHRONY_CONF_D" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        CONF_FILES+=("$f")
    done < <(find "$CHRONY_CONF_D" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

if [ ${#CONF_FILES[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No chrony configuration files found; installing chrony configuration is out of scope." >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

for conf in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -w "$conf" ] || { echo "Cannot write to $conf, skipping." >&2; continue; }

    # Create a working copy.
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    cp -a --preserve=all "$conf" "$tmp"

    # Process each line: if the (non-comment, non-blank) line starts with
    # server, pool, or peer, ensure maxpoll <val> is present. If maxpoll
    # already exists, normalize it to the desired value.
    awk -v mp="$MAXPOLL_VALUE" '
    {
        line = $0
        # Preserve blank/comment lines unchanged.
        stripped = line
        sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, "", stripped)
        if (stripped == "" || substr(stripped,1,1) == "#") {
            print line
            next
        }

        # Split leading whitespace from directive.
        match(line, /^[[:space:]]*/)
        lead = substr(line, 1, RLENGTH)
        rest = substr(line, RLENGTH + 1)

        # First token is the directive.
        n = split(rest, toks, /[[:space:]]+/)
        directive = tolower(toks[1])

        if (directive == "server" || directive == "pool" || directive == "peer") {
            # Separate any trailing inline comment.
            comment = ""
            body = rest
            hashidx = index(rest, "#")
            if (hashidx > 0) {
                body = substr(rest, 1, hashidx - 1)
                comment = substr(rest, hashidx)
                sub(/[[:space:]]+$/, "", body)
            }

            if (match(body, /(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/)) {
                # Replace existing maxpoll value.
                newbody = ""
                remaining = body
                while (match(remaining, /(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/)) {
                    pre = substr(remaining, 1, RSTART - 1)
                    matched = substr(remaining, RSTART, RLENGTH)
                    # Preserve any leading whitespace inside the match.
                    lead_ws = ""
                    if (match(matched, /^[[:space:]]+/)) {
                        lead_ws = substr(matched, 1, RLENGTH)
                    }
                    newbody = newbody pre lead_ws "maxpoll " mp
                    remaining = substr(remaining, RSTART + RLENGTH)
                }
                newbody = newbody remaining
                body = newbody
            } else {
                # Append maxpoll.
                body = body " maxpoll " mp
            }

            if (comment != "") {
                print lead body " " comment
            } else {
                print lead body
            }
        } else {
            print line
        }
    }
    ' "$conf" > "$tmp"

    if ! cmp -s "$conf" "$tmp"; then
        backup="${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        cp -a --preserve=all "$conf" "$backup"
        cat "$tmp" > "$conf"
        echo "Updated $conf (backup: $backup)"
        changed=1
    fi
    rm -f "$tmp"
done

# Validate resulting configuration: every server/pool/peer must now have maxpoll.
missing=0
for conf in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        stripped="${line#"${line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
        [ -z "$stripped" ] && continue
        case "$stripped" in
            \#*) continue ;;
        esac
        first="$(echo "$stripped" | awk '{print tolower($1)}')"
        case "$first" in
            server|pool|peer)
                # Strip inline comment.
                nocomment="${stripped%%#*}"
                if ! echo " $nocomment " | grep -Eq '[[:space:]]maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+'; then
                    echo "ERROR: line without maxpoll in $conf: $line" >&2
                    missing=1
                fi
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$conf"
done

if [ "$missing" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "One or more time sources still lack an explicit maxpoll setting." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Reload/restart chronyd if it is active so the changes take effect.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd; then
            systemctl restart chronyd || echo "Warning: failed to restart chronyd." >&2
        fi
        systemctl enable chronyd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    echo "Chrony configuration updated: all time sources now have maxpoll=${MAXPOLL_VALUE}."
else
    echo "All time sources already have an explicit maxpoll value; no changes made."
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Configure pam_faillock to operate silently to prevent
# username enumeration via differential feedback.
#

set -u

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure the faillock.conf file exists
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chown root:root "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Ensure 'silent' is set (uncommented) in /etc/security/faillock.conf
if grep -Eq '^\s*#\s*silent\s*$' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    sed -i 's/^\s*#\s*silent\s*$/silent/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
elif ! grep -Eq '^\s*silent\s*$' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    echo "silent" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Ensure authselect profile carries the setting through PAM stacks
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk -F'Profile ID: ' '/Profile ID:/ {print $2}' | tr -d ' ')

    if [ -z "${CURRENT_PROFILE:-}" ]; then
        # Create/select a custom profile based on sssd if none is selected
        authselect create-profile hardening -b sssd --symlink-meta >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        authselect select custom/hardening with-faillock --force >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            authselect select sssd with-faillock --force >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        # Ensure with-faillock feature is enabled in the currently selected profile
        if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
                authselect select "$CURRENT_PROFILE" with-faillock --force >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi
    fi

    authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Fallback: ensure PAM files also carry 'silent' on pam_faillock lines
# (in case authselect is not managing them or for redundancy in system-auth and password-auth)
for PAMFILE in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    [ -f "$PAMFILE" ] || continue

    # Skip if managed by authselect (symlink) — authselect handles this via faillock.conf
    if [ -L "$PAMFILE" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Add 'silent' to any pam_faillock line that doesn't already have it
    awk '
    /pam_faillock\.so/ {
        if ($0 !~ /[[:space:]]silent([[:space:]]|$)/) {
            $0 = $0 " silent"
        }
    }
    { print }
    ' "$PAMFILE" > "${PAMFILE}.tmp" && mv "${PAMFILE}.tmp" "$PAMFILE"
    chmod 0644 "$PAMFILE"
    chown root:root "$PAMFILE"
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Determine architecture
if grep -q 'aarch64\|x86_64\|ppc64\|s390x' <<< "$(uname -m)"; then
    ARCH64=true
else
    ARCH64=false
fi

# Function to add rule to a file if not present
add_rule_to_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    
    if [ ! -e "$file" ]; then
        touch "$file"
    fi
    
    # Check if the exact rule exists
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$file"; then
        # Remove any conflicting mount rules with the same key but different specs
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Rules files
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/rules.d/mount.rules"
AUDIT_CONF="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Build the rules
RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=export"
RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=export"

# Remove any existing mount audit rules with export key to avoid duplicates (with variations)
for f in "$AUDIT_RULES" "$AUDIT_CONF"; do
    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
        # Create a temp file, filter out existing mount export rules
        sed -i '/-S[[:space:]]*mount.*key=export/d; /-S[[:space:]]*mount.*-k[[:space:]]*export/d' "$f" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

# Add rules to /etc/audit/rules.d/mount.rules
add_rule_to_file "$AUDIT_RULES" "$RULE_B32"
if [ "$ARCH64" = true ]; then
    add_rule_to_file "$AUDIT_RULES" "$RULE_B64"
fi

# Also add to /etc/audit/audit.rules for immediate persistence if needed
add_rule_to_file "$AUDIT_CONF" "$RULE_B32"
if [ "$ARCH64" = true ]; then
    add_rule_to_file "$AUDIT_CONF" "$RULE_B64"
fi

# Load rules
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

# Restart auditd using service (auditd doesn't allow systemctl restart)
if service auditd status &>/dev/null; then
    service auditd restart || true
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure rsyslog forwards logs over encrypted (TLS) transport.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.
#

set -u

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_CONFD="/etc/rsyslog.d"
TLS_DROPIN="${RSYSLOG_CONFD}/99-tls-forwarding.conf"

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y rsyslog || yum install -y rsyslog
fi

# Ensure gtls driver package is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls || yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

# Locate all rsyslog config files
CONF_FILES=("${RSYSLOG_CONF}")
if [ -d "${RSYSLOG_CONFD}" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        CONF_FILES+=("$f")
    done < <(find "${RSYSLOG_CONFD}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0)
fi

# Comment out any unencrypted forwarding lines:
#   @host       -> UDP (unencrypted)
#   @@host      -> TCP without TLS (unencrypted)
# We leave lines that already use TLS (e.g., action(... type="omfwd" ... StreamDriverMode="1" ...))
# and preserve any already-commented lines.
for f in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue

    # Skip our own drop-in
    [ "$f" = "${TLS_DROPIN}" ] && continue

    # Comment out legacy forwarding directives that are NOT TLS:
    #   Lines starting with *.* @host or @@host (single @ = UDP, @@ = plain TCP)
    # We only comment when NOT already commented.
    sed -ri.bak-remediation \
        -e 's|^([[:space:]]*[^#[:space:]].*[[:space:]]@[^@[:space:]#]+.*)$|#\1  # disabled: unencrypted forwarding|' \
        "$f"

    # Comment out action(type="omfwd" ...) lines that do NOT set StreamDriverMode="1"
    # (Best effort using awk; only if line is a single-line action definition)
    awk '
    {
        line=$0
        if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*action\(/ && line ~ /type="omfwd"/ && line !~ /StreamDriverMode="1"/ && line !~ /^[[:space:]]*#/) {
            print "# disabled unencrypted forwarding: " line
        } else {
            print line
        }
    }' "$f" > "${f}.tmp.$$" && mv "${f}.tmp.$$" "$f"
done

# Ensure TLS CA certificate path is configurable. We use a sane default that
# an administrator can override; if it does not exist, we still write config
# (rsyslog will log the error, but the intent of enforcing TLS is preserved).
CA_CERT="${RSYSLOG_TLS_CA:-/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt}"
REMOTE_HOST="${RSYSLOG_REMOTE_HOST:-loghost.example.com}"
REMOTE_PORT="${RSYSLOG_REMOTE_PORT:-6514}"

# Write / update the TLS forwarding drop-in configuration (idempotent).
TMP_DROPIN="$(mktemp)"
cat > "${TMP_DROPIN}" <<EOF
# Managed by remediation script - enforce encrypted (TLS) log forwarding.
# Do not edit by hand; adjust variables and re-run the remediation script.

global(
    DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile="${CA_CERT}"
)

module(
    load="omfwd"
)

action(
    type="omfwd"
    Target="${REMOTE_HOST}"
    Port="${REMOTE_PORT}"
    Protocol="tcp"
    StreamDriver="gtls"
    StreamDriverMode="1"
    StreamDriverAuthMode="x509/name"
    StreamDriverPermittedPeers="${REMOTE_HOST}"
    queue.type="LinkedList"
    queue.filename="fwd_tls_queue"
    queue.maxdiskspace="1g"
    queue.saveonshutdown="on"
    action.resumeRetryCount="-1"
)
EOF

if [ ! -f "${TLS_DROPIN}" ] || ! cmp -s "${TMP_DROPIN}" "${TLS_DROPIN}"; then
    install -m 0600 -o root -g root "${TMP_DROPIN}" "${TLS_DROPIN}"
    CHANGED=1
else
    CHANGED=0
fi
rm -f "${TMP_DROPIN}"

# Ensure permissions are locked down
chmod 0600 "${TLS_DROPIN}"
chown root:root "${TLS_DROPIN}"

# Ensure rsyslog is enabled at boot (persist across reboots)
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Validate configuration and restart if changed or if service is not active
if rsyslogd -N1 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ] || ! systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
        systemctl restart rsyslog
    fi
else
    echo "ERROR: rsyslog configuration validation failed. Not restarting service." >&2
    rsyslogd -N1 || true
    exit 1
fi

echo "Encrypted (TLS) log forwarding is enforced via ${TLS_DROPIN}."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enable faillock even_deny_root on RHEL 8
# Ensures root is subject to the same lockout policy as other accounts.
#

set -o pipefail

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure faillock.conf exists
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chown root:root "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Set even_deny_root in /etc/security/faillock.conf idempotently
if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*even_deny_root\b' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    sed -i 's|^\s*#\?\s*even_deny_root.*|even_deny_root|' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
    echo "even_deny_root" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Ensure authselect profile enforces faillock with-faillock feature
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk -F': ' '/Profile ID/ {print $2}')
    if [ -z "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]; then
        # No current profile; select sssd with faillock
        authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
    else
        # Enable with-faillock feature if not already enabled
        if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'with-faillock'; then
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        fi
        authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
fi

# As a safety net, also ensure PAM files reference pam_faillock and even_deny_root
# in case authselect is not in use.
for PAM_FILE in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    [ -f "$PAM_FILE" ] || continue

    # Only modify files that are not symlinks managed by authselect
    if [ -L "$PAM_FILE" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Ensure pam_faillock preauth line exists
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+preauth' "$PAM_FILE"; then
        sed -i '/^auth\s\+.*pam_unix\.so/i auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent' "$PAM_FILE"
    fi

    # Ensure pam_faillock authfail line exists
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+.*pam_faillock\.so\s+authfail' "$PAM_FILE"; then
        sed -i '/^auth\s\+.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail' "$PAM_FILE"
    fi

    # Ensure account line exists
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*account\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so' "$PAM_FILE"; then
        sed -i '/^account\s\+.*pam_unix\.so/i account     required                                     pam_faillock.so' "$PAM_FILE"
    fi

    # Ensure even_deny_root appears on faillock lines (for older PAM syntax that puts opts inline)
    if grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+.*pam_faillock\.so' "$PAM_FILE" && ! grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+.*pam_faillock\.so.*even_deny_root' "$PAM_FILE"; then
        # only add if the file uses inline options style; otherwise faillock.conf handles it
        :
    fi
done

exit 0

install -d -m 0755 /etc/dconf/profile

    cat > "$PROFILE_FILE" <<'EOF'

system-db:local
EOF
    chmod 0644 "$PROFILE_FILE"
fi

SETTINGS_FILE="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-security-settings"
KEY_PATH="/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard"
KEY_NAME="removal-action"
KEY_VALUE="'lock-screen'"

if [ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]; then
    cat > "$SETTINGS_FILE" <<EOF
[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]
removal-action='lock-screen'
EOF

    if grep -q '^\[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard\]' "$SETTINGS_FILE"; then

import sys, re
path = sys.argv[1]
with open(path) as f:
    content = f.read()

section = '[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]'
pattern = re.compile(r'(\[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard\][^\[]*)', re.DOTALL)
m = pattern.search(content)
if m:
    block = m.group(1)
    if re.search(r'^removal-action\s*=', block, re.MULTILINE):
        new_block = re.sub(r'^removal-action\s*=.*$', "removal-action='lock-screen'", block, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    else:
        # Add the key at end of block
        new_block = block.rstrip() + "\nremoval-action='lock-screen'\n"
    content = content.replace(block, new_block)
else:
    content += "\n[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]\nremoval-action='lock-screen'\n"

with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
PYEOF
    else
        printf '\n[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]\nremoval-action=%s\n' "$KEY_VALUE" >> "$SETTINGS_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Lock the setting so users cannot override it

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Ensure GNOME dconf directories exist
install -d -m 0755 /etc/dconf/db/local.d
install -d -m 0755 /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks
install -d -m 0755 /etc/dconf/profile

# Ensure the user profile references the local database
PROFILE_FILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"
if [ ! -f "$PROFILE_FILE" ] || ! grep -q '^system-db:local' "$PROFILE_FILE"; then
    cat > "$PROFILE_FILE" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
    chmod 0644 "$PROFILE_FILE"
fi

# Configure the smartcard removal action
SETTINGS_FILE="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-security-settings"
KEY_PATH="/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard"
KEY_NAME="removal-action"
KEY_VALUE="'lock-screen'"

if [ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]; then
    cat > "$SETTINGS_FILE" <<EOF
[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]
removal-action='lock-screen'
EOF
else
    if grep -q '^\[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard\]' "$SETTINGS_FILE"; then
        # Section exists; ensure key is set correctly
        python3 - "$SETTINGS_FILE" <<'PYEOF'
import sys, re
path = sys.argv[1]
with open(path) as f:
    content = f.read()

section = '[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]'
pattern = re.compile(r'(\[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard\][^\[]*)', re.DOTALL)
m = pattern.search(content)
if m:
    block = m.group(1)
    if re.search(r'^removal-action\s*=', block, re.MULTILINE):
        new_block = re.sub(r'^removal-action\s*=.*$', "removal-action='lock-screen'", block, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    else:
        # Add the key at end of block
        new_block = block.rstrip() + "\nremoval-action='lock-screen'\n"
    content = content.replace(block, new_block)
else:
    content += "\n[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]\nremoval-action='lock-screen'\n"

with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
PYEOF
    else
        printf '\n[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]\nremoval-action=%s\n' "$KEY_VALUE" >> "$SETTINGS_FILE"
    fi
fi
chmod 0644 "$SETTINGS_FILE"

# Lock the setting so users cannot override it
LOCK_FILE="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/00-security-settings.lock"
LOCK_LINE="/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard/removal-action"

if [ ! -f "$LOCK_FILE" ] || ! grep -qxF "$LOCK_LINE" "$LOCK_FILE"; then
    echo "$LOCK_LINE" >> "$LOCK_FILE"
fi
chmod 0644 "$LOCK_FILE"

# Update the dconf system databases
if command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dconf update
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -u

MAX_DAYS=365

# Update the default for new accounts in /etc/login.defs
if [ -f /etc/login.defs ]; then
    if grep -qE '^\s*PASS_MAX_DAYS\s+' /etc/login.defs; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*PASS_MAX_DAYS\s+.*|PASS_MAX_DAYS   ${MAX_DAYS}|" /etc/login.defs
    else
        echo "PASS_MAX_DAYS   ${MAX_DAYS}" >> /etc/login.defs
    fi
fi

# Iterate over local users in /etc/passwd and enforce PASS_MAX_DAYS
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ _ shell; do
    # Skip system accounts and accounts with nologin/false shells? 
    # The requirement is all existing local user accounts. Apply broadly, but
    # skip accounts that cannot log in via password shells like /sbin/nologin? 
    # To be safe and cover all local accounts (including root), apply to all.
    
    # Skip if user does not exist in shadow (shouldn't happen but be defensive)
    if ! getent shadow "$username" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        continue
    fi
    
    current_max=$(chage -l "$username" 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '/Maximum number of days between password change/ {gsub(/ /,"",$2); print $2}')
    
    if [ -z "$current_max" ]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # If current max is "never" or greater than MAX_DAYS or -1, set it
    if [ "$current_max" = "never" ] || [ "$current_max" = "-1" ]; then
        chage -M "$MAX_DAYS" "$username"
    elif [ "$current_max" -gt "$MAX_DAYS" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        chage -M "$MAX_DAYS" "$username"
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

    [ -f "$f" ] || continue

    fi
done

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

# The syscall in question is fsetxattr
# Key tag for permission modification events: perm_mod

# Determine which rules file to use based on augenrules vs auditctl
if [ -e /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules ] || [ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]; then
    USES_AUGENRULES=true
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
else
    USES_AUGENRULES=false
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
fi

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Function to add a rule to a file if not present
add_rule_if_missing() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    
    # Create file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        touch "$file"
        chmod 0640 "$file"
    fi
    
    # Check if the rule already exists (matching key syscall and auid pattern)
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$file"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Remove any existing fsetxattr rules from all rule files to avoid duplicates/conflicts
clean_existing_fsetxattr() {
    local files=()
    if $USES_AUGENRULES; then
        files=(/etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules)
    else
        files=(/etc/audit/audit.rules)
    fi
    
    for f in "${files[@]}"; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        # Remove lines that contain fsetxattr syscall audit rules
        sed -i '/-S[[:space:]]*fsetxattr\|-S.*,fsetxattr\|fsetxattr,/d' "$f" 2>/dev/null || true
    done
}

# Determine target file
if $USES_AUGENRULES; then
    TARGET_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
else
    TARGET_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
fi

# Clean up any existing fsetxattr rules
clean_existing_fsetxattr

# Define the four required rules
RULE_B32_UNPRIV="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_B32_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fsetxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_B64_UNPRIV="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_B64_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"

add_rule_if_missing "$TARGET_FILE" "$RULE_B32_UNPRIV"
add_rule_if_missing "$TARGET_FILE" "$RULE_B32_ROOT"
add_rule_if_missing "$TARGET_FILE" "$RULE_B64_UNPRIV"
add_rule_if_missing "$TARGET_FILE" "$RULE_B64_ROOT"

chmod 0640 "$TARGET_FILE"

# Reload audit rules
if $USES_AUGENRULES; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load || true
    fi
else
    if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules || true
    fi
fi

# If auditd requires restart to pick up changes and is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Enable Kernel Page-Table Isolation (pti=on) via bootloader for Meltdown mitigation

# Update GRUB default config for future kernel installs and grub regeneration
if [ -f /etc/default/grub ]; then
    if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" /etc/default/grub; then
        if grep -q "pti=" /etc/default/grub; then
            sed -i 's/\bpti=[^"[:space:]]*/pti=on/g' /etc/default/grub
        else
            sed -i 's/^\(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="[^"]*\)"/\1 pti=on"/' /etc/default/grub
        fi
    else
        echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="pti=on"' >> /etc/default/grub
    fi
fi

# Apply to all currently installed kernels via grubby
if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="pti=on"
fi

# Regenerate GRUB config to ensure persistence
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG=$(find /boot/efi/EFI -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    [ -z "$GRUB_CFG" ] && GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

if command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -n "$GRUB_CFG" ]; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "$GRUB_CFG" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enable pam_faillock for account lockout on RHEL 8
# Ensures failed login attempts are tracked via pam_faillock in the
# system-auth and password-auth PAM stacks.
#

set -o pipefail

PAM_FILES=(
    "/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
    "/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
)

# Ensure faillock.conf exists and has sensible persistent settings
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

PARAM="net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects"
VALUE=0

# Apply at runtime
sysctl -w ${PARAM}=${VALUE} >/dev/null
sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=${VALUE} >/dev/null

# Ensure persistence: remove any conflicting settings in existing sysctl files
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do

#!/bin/bash

# The syscall for changing ownership via open file descriptor is fchown
# Key identifier for permission modification events: perm_mod

# Function to add rule to a file if not present
add_rule() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Rules for augenrules (persistent) - /etc/audit/rules.d/
RULES_D_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
touch "$RULES_D_FILE"

RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"

# Remove any existing fchown rules that might conflict, then add ours
# Check for existing rules with different formatting
if ! grep -qE '^-a\s+always,exit.*arch=b64.*-S\s+fchown.*auid>=1000.*key=perm_mod' "$RULES_D_FILE"; then
    # Remove any partial/conflicting fchown b64 rules
    sed -i '/^-a.*arch=b64.*fchown/d' "$RULES_D_FILE"
    echo "$RULE_64" >> "$RULES_D_FILE"
fi

if ! grep -qE '^-a\s+always,exit.*arch=b32.*-S\s+fchown.*auid>=1000.*key=perm_mod' "$RULES_D_FILE"; then
    sed -i '/^-a.*arch=b32.*fchown/d' "$RULES_D_FILE"
    echo "$RULE_32" >> "$RULES_D_FILE"
fi

# Also update /etc/audit/audit.rules for auditctl mechanism
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
touch "$AUDIT_RULES"

if ! grep -qE '^-a\s+always,exit.*arch=b64.*-S\s+fchown.*auid>=1000.*key=perm_mod' "$AUDIT_RULES"; then
    sed -i '/^-a.*arch=b64.*fchown/d' "$AUDIT_RULES"
    echo "$RULE_64" >> "$AUDIT_RULES"
fi

if ! grep -qE '^-a\s+always,exit.*arch=b32.*-S\s+fchown.*auid>=1000.*key=perm_mod' "$AUDIT_RULES"; then
    sed -i '/^-a.*arch=b32.*fchown/d' "$AUDIT_RULES"
    echo "$RULE_32" >> "$AUDIT_RULES"
fi

# Load the rules
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
fi

# Signal auditd to reload
if systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then
    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable SSH user environment processing (PermitUserEnvironment no)
# Target: RHEL 8
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times
#

set -o pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/00-disable-user-env.conf"
DESIRED_KEY="PermitUserEnvironment"
DESIRED_VALUE="no"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SSHD_CONFIG} does not exist. Is openssh-server installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# 1. Normalize main sshd_config: remove any existing PermitUserEnvironment lines
#    (commented or not) and append the correct directive at the end.
if grep -qiE '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*PermitUserEnvironment\b' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    # Remove all existing occurrences (commented and uncommented)
    sed -i.bak -E "/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*PermitUserEnvironment\b.*$/d" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
fi

# Append the authoritative setting
echo "${DESIRED_KEY} ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# 2. Create a drop-in file to ensure the setting is not overridden by other
#    files in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/. On RHEL 8, the main sshd_config does
#    NOT include drop-in files by default, but we create one for defense-in-depth
#    in case a local admin added an Include directive or on systems where it's used.
if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
    # Remove any conflicting settings from other drop-in files
    for f in "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}"/*.conf; do
        [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
        [[ "$f" == "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]] && continue
        if grep -qiE '^[[:space:]]*PermitUserEnvironment\b' "$f"; then
            sed -i.bak -E "/^[[:space:]]*PermitUserEnvironment\b.*$/d" "$f"
        fi
    done

    # Write our authoritative drop-in
    cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<EOF
# Managed by remediation script
# Prevent users from supplying/overriding environment variables via SSH
${DESIRED_KEY} ${DESIRED_VALUE}
EOF
    chmod 0600 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
    chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"
fi

# 3. Validate the resulting SSH configuration
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd configuration test failed. Review ${SSHD_CONFIG} and ${DROPIN_FILE}." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# 4. Reload sshd to apply changes (only if it's active)
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
fi

# 5. Verify the effective setting
EFFECTIVE_VALUE="$(sshd -T 2>/dev/null | awk 'tolower($1)=="permituserenvironment"{print tolower($2)}')"
if [[ "${EFFECTIVE_VALUE}" != "no" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Effective PermitUserEnvironment is '${EFFECTIVE_VALUE}', expected 'no'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "OK: PermitUserEnvironment is set to 'no' and sshd configuration is valid."
exit 0

#
# Remediation: Ensure systemd debug-shell service is disabled and masked.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -o pipefail

UNIT="debug-shell.service"

if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: systemctl not found; this system does not appear to use systemd." >&2
    exit 1
fi

rc=0

# Stop the service if it is currently active
if systemctl is-active --quiet "${UNIT}"; then
    echo "Stopping ${UNIT}..."
    systemctl stop "${UNIT}" || rc=$?
else
    echo "${UNIT} is not active."
fi

# Disable the service so it does not start at boot
if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Disabling ${UNIT}..."
    systemctl disable "${UNIT}" || rc=$?
else
    echo "${UNIT} is not enabled."
fi

# Mask the service so it cannot be started (manually or via dependency)
current_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "${current_state}" != "masked" ]; then
    echo "Masking ${UNIT}..."
    systemctl mask "${UNIT}" || rc=$?
else
    echo "${UNIT} is already masked."
fi

# Reload systemd to make sure changes are picked up
systemctl daemon-reload || rc=$?

# Verify final state
final_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
active_state="$(systemctl is-active "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null || true)"

echo "Final state: is-enabled=${final_state}, is-active=${active_state}"

if [ "${final_state}" = "masked" ] && [ "${active_state}" != "active" ]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${UNIT} is disabled and masked."
    exit 0
else
    echo "FAILURE: ${UNIT} could not be fully disabled/masked." >&2
    exit "${rc:-1}"
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Remediate permissions on non-hidden files/directories within local interactive users' home directories.
# Ensures mode is no more permissive than 0750.

set -u

# Determine UID thresholds from /etc/login.defs
UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN\s+/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)
UID_MAX=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MAX\s+/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)
UID_MIN=${UID_MIN:-1000}
UID_MAX=${UID_MAX:-60000}

# Get list of local interactive users from /etc/passwd
while IFS=: read -r user _ uid _ _ home shell; do
    # Skip if UID out of range
    [ "$uid" -ge "$UID_MIN" ] 2>/dev/null || continue
    [ "$uid" -le "$UID_MAX" ] 2>/dev/null || continue

    # Skip non-interactive shells
    case "$shell" in
        */nologin|*/false|/bin/sync|/sbin/shutdown|/sbin/halt|"")
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Skip if home doesn't exist or isn't a directory
    [ -d "$home" ] || continue

    # Skip system home directories
    case "$home" in
        /|/root|/dev/null|/sbin/nologin|/bin/sync)
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Iterate over non-hidden entries in the home directory
    shopt -s nullglob
    for entry in "$home"/*; do
        # Recursively adjust permissions on the entry (files and directories),
        # excluding hidden entries at the top level (already excluded by glob).
        # Strip world permissions and group write/special from all items within.
        find "$entry" -exec chmod u-s,g-ws,o-rwx {} \; 2>/dev/null
    done
    shopt -u nullglob

done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce minimum password length of 15 characters system-wide
# Target: RHEL 8
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times
#

set -o pipefail

MIN_LEN=15
PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_CONF_D="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"

# Ensure pwquality.conf exists
if [ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    chown root:root "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Set minlen in /etc/security/pwquality.conf idempotently
if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*minlen\s*=' "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    # Replace any existing (commented or uncommented) minlen line with the desired value
    sed -i -E "s|^\s*#?\s*minlen\s*=.*|minlen = ${MIN_LEN}|g" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
else
    printf '\n# Set by remediation script: enforce minimum password length\nminlen = %s\n' "${MIN_LEN}" >> "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Also handle any drop-in files under /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/ that might override the setting
if [ -d "${PWQUALITY_CONF_D}" ]; then
    for f in "${PWQUALITY_CONF_D}"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*minlen\s*=' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E "s|^\s*#?\s*minlen\s*=.*|minlen = ${MIN_LEN}|g" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Ensure PAM configurations reference pam_pwquality (RHEL 8 default via authselect)
# Update password-auth and system-auth to remove any conflicting/weaker minlen argument on pam_pwquality
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [ -f "${pam_file}" ]; then
        # If pam_pwquality has an inline minlen= argument, remove it so pwquality.conf takes effect
        if grep -Eq '^\s*password\s+.*pam_pwquality\.so.*\sminlen=' "${pam_file}"; then
            sed -i -E 's|(^\s*password\s+.*pam_pwquality\.so.*)\sminlen=[0-9]+|\1|g' "${pam_file}"
        fi
    fi
done

# Verify the setting
current="$(grep -E '^\s*minlen\s*=' "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" | tail -n1 | awk -F'=' '{gsub(/ /,"",$2); print $2}')"
if [ "${current}" = "${MIN_LEN}" ]; then
    echo "Success: minlen is set to ${MIN_LEN} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: failed to enforce minlen=${MIN_LEN} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF} (found: '${current}')" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure fapolicyd for deny-all, permit-by-exception application allowlisting.
# Idempotent remediation script for RHEL 8.

set -euo pipefail

# Must run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure fapolicyd package is installed
if ! rpm -q fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing fapolicyd..."
    dnf install -y fapolicyd
else
    echo "fapolicyd package already installed."
fi

CONF_FILE="/etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.conf"

# Ensure configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Configuration file $CONF_FILE missing; reinstalling fapolicyd." >&2
    dnf reinstall -y fapolicyd
fi

# Helper to set a key=value in fapolicyd.conf idempotently
set_conf() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "$file"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key} = ${value}|" "$file"
    else
        echo "${key} = ${value}" >> "$file"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure fapolicyd for deny-all, permit-by-exception application allowlisting.
# Idempotent remediation script for RHEL 8.

set -euo pipefail

# Must run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure fapolicyd package is installed
if ! rpm -q fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing fapolicyd..."
    dnf install -y fapolicyd
else
    echo "fapolicyd package already installed."
fi

CONF_FILE="/etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.conf"

# Ensure configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Configuration file $CONF_FILE missing; reinstalling fapolicyd." >&2
    dnf reinstall -y fapolicyd
fi

# Helper to set a key=value in fapolicyd.conf idempotently
set_conf() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "$file"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key} = ${value}|" "$file"
    else
        echo "${key} = ${value}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Enforce permissive = 0 (enforcing mode = deny by default)
set_conf "permissive" "0" "$CONF_FILE"

# Ensure integrity checking is on (sha256 recommended)
if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*integrity[[:space:]]*=" "$CONF_FILE"; then
    echo "integrity = sha256" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Determine rules location (RHEL 8.x uses /etc/fapolicyd/rules.d/, older used compiled.rules / fapolicyd.rules)
RULES_D="/etc/fapolicyd/rules.d"
LEGACY_RULES="/etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.rules"

if [[ -d "$RULES_D" ]]; then
    # Modern layout - ensure a final default-deny rule exists
    DENY_FILE="${RULES_D}/99-deny-all.rules"
    DENY_LINE="deny_audit perm=any all : all"
    if [[ ! -f "$DENY_FILE" ]] || ! grep -Fxq "$DENY_LINE" "$DENY_FILE"; then
        echo "$DENY_LINE" > "$DENY_FILE"
        chown root:fapolicyd "$DENY_FILE" 2>/dev/null || chown root:root "$DENY_FILE"
        chmod 0644 "$DENY_FILE"
    fi
    # Regenerate compiled rules if fagenrules is available
    if command -v fagenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fagenrules --load || true
    fi
elif [[ -f "$LEGACY_RULES" ]]; then
    DENY_LINE="deny_audit perm=any all : all"
    if ! grep -Fxq "$DENY_LINE" "$LEGACY_RULES"; then
        echo "$DENY_LINE" >> "$LEGACY_RULES"
    fi
fi

# Enable the service so it persists across reboots
systemctl enable fapolicyd.service

# Start or restart to apply configuration
if systemctl is-active --quiet fapolicyd.service; then
    systemctl restart fapolicyd.service
else
    systemctl start fapolicyd.service
fi

# Verify status
systemctl --no-pager status fapolicyd.service || true

echo "fapolicyd configured for deny-all, permit-by-exception (enforcing) mode."

set -euo pipefail

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"
CONFIG_DIR="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends"

# Ensure directory exists
mkdir -p "$CONFIG_DIR"

# If the file is a symlink to the system policy, that's the desired state.
# Verify it doesn't contain any disallowed protocol enables.
# Disallowed protocols
DISALLOWED=('SSL3.0' 'TLS1.0' 'TLS1.1' 'DTLS0.9' 'DTLS1.0')

# If gnutls.config exists (regular file or symlink target), remove any lines
# that enable insecure protocols via +VERS-*
clean_file() {
    local file="$1"
    # Resolve symlink target for modification if needed
    local target
    target=$(readlink -f "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "$file")

    if [ -f "$target" ]; then
        # Create a temp file for modifications
        local tmp
        tmp=$(mktemp)
        cp "$target" "$tmp"

        # Remove +VERS-SSL3.0, +VERS-TLS1.0, +VERS-TLS1.1, +VERS-DTLS0.9, +VERS-DTLS1.0
        sed -i -E 's/\+VERS-SSL3\.0//g;
                   s/\+VERS-TLS1\.0//g;
                   s/\+VERS-TLS1\.1//g;
                   s/\+VERS-DTLS0\.9//g;
                   s/\+VERS-DTLS1\.0//g' "$tmp"

        # Ensure -VERS-* entries exist for disallowed protocols in min-verification style
        # Actually the correct approach: rely on system policy. We only ensure the file
        # doesn't override with enables.

        if ! cmp -s "$tmp" "$target"; then
            cp "$tmp" "$target"
        fi
        rm -f "$tmp"
    fi
}

# If the file doesn't exist or isn't a symlink, restore from update-crypto-policies
if [ ! -e "$CONFIG_FILE" ] || [ ! -L "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    # Remove any regular file that overrides the policy symlink
    if [ -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ] && [ ! -L "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
        rm -f "$CONFIG_FILE"
    fi
    # Regenerate crypto policy back-end links
    if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Clean any disallowed protocol enables from the effective config file
clean_file "$CONFIG_FILE"

# Verify no disallowed protocols are enabled
if [ -e "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    for proto in "${DISALLOWED[@]}"; do
        if grep -Eq "\+VERS-${proto//./\\.}" "$CONFIG_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "Warning: $proto still enabled in $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
        fi
    done
fi

echo "GnuTLS back-end configured to honor system crypto policy."

    sed -i '\|-F path=/usr/sbin/semanage|d' "$f"

echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> "$RULES_FILE"

# Load rules into the running audit daemon
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

# Reload audit daemon

#
# Ensure auditd logs modifications to /etc/sudoers.d
#

set -u

RULE='-w /etc/sudoers.d/ -p wa -k identity'

# Persistent rules file (loaded by augenrules)
RULES_D_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
# Direct rules file (loaded by auditctl)
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

ensure_rule_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    # Create the file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "$file")"
        touch "$file"
        chmod 0640 "$file"
    fi

    # Look for an existing watch on /etc/sudoers.d/ (any form) and normalize it
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+/etc/sudoers\.d/?[[:space:]]' "$file"; then
        # Replace any existing line watching /etc/sudoers.d with the canonical rule
        sed -i -E "\|^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+/etc/sudoers\.d/?[[:space:]].*|c\\${rule}" "$file"
    else
        printf '%s\n' "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

ensure_rule_in_file "$RULES_D_FILE" "$RULE"
ensure_rule_in_file "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "$RULE"

# Reload audit rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# If auditd is running with immutable flag (-e 2), rules require a reboot to load.
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '^-w[[:space:]]+/etc/sudoers\.d/?[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+identity'; then
        auditctl -w /etc/sudoers.d/ -p wa -k identity >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure SSH server uses system-wide crypto policy for ciphers
# and that the crypto-policy backend contains the correct FIPS 140-2
# validated cipher list (strongest-to-weakest), uncommented.
#
set -o pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
CRYPTO_BACKEND="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"
CIPHER_LINE='Ciphers aes256-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes128-ctr,aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-gcm@openssh.com'

# 1. Remove any local override of Ciphers in /etc/ssh/sshd_config so that
#    the SSH server inherits the setting from the crypto-policy backend.
if [ -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    # Remove active (uncommented) Ciphers lines
    if grep -qiE '^[[:space:]]*Ciphers[[:space:]]+' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        sed -i.bak -r '/^[[:space:]]*[Cc]iphers[[:space:]]+/d' "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

# Also remove Ciphers overrides from any drop-in files under /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/
if [ -d /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -qiE '^[[:space:]]*Ciphers[[:space:]]+' "$f"; then
            sed -i.bak -r '/^[[:space:]]*[Cc]iphers[[:space:]]+/d' "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# 2. Ensure the crypto-policies openssh server back-end file exists and
#    contains the correct Ciphers line, uncommented and consolidated.
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$CRYPTO_BACKEND")"

if [ ! -f "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" ]; then
    touch "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
fi

# If the file is a symlink managed by update-crypto-policies, replace it
# with a concrete file so we can enforce content deterministically, but
# first capture existing contents.
if [ -L "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" ]; then
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    cat "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" > "$tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
    mv "$tmp" "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
    chmod 0644 "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
fi

# Remove all existing Ciphers lines (commented or not) to avoid fragmentation
sed -i.bak -r '/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*Ciphers[[:space:]]+/d' "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"

# Append the canonical, uncommented Ciphers line
printf '%s\n' "$CIPHER_LINE" >> "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"

# Normalize: strip duplicate blank lines
awk 'NF||p{print} {p=NF}' "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" > "${CRYPTO_BACKEND}.tmp" && \
    mv "${CRYPTO_BACKEND}.tmp" "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"

chown root:root "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
chmod 0644 "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"

# 3. Ensure the system crypto policy is applied so that the change persists
if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_POLICY="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null || echo DEFAULT)"
    # Re-apply current policy (this regenerates back-ends; our concrete file
    # written above overrides symlink management for the Ciphers directive).
    update-crypto-policies --set "$CURRENT_POLICY" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # After re-apply, ensure our Ciphers line is still present/uncommented.
    if [ -L "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" ] || ! grep -qE "^${CIPHER_LINE}$" "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" 2>/dev/null; then
        if [ -L "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" ]; then
            tmp="$(mktemp)"
            cat "$CRYPTO_BACKEND" > "$tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
            rm -f "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
            mv "$tmp" "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
        fi
        sed -i -r '/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*Ciphers[[:space:]]+/d' "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
        printf '%s\n' "$CIPHER_LINE" >> "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
        chown root:root "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
        chmod 0644 "$CRYPTO_BACKEND"
    fi
fi

# 4. Validate sshd configuration and reload if valid
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if sshd -t >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
            systemctl reload sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl restart sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Enable OCSP certificate verification in SSSD
# for smart-card / certificate-based authentication on RHEL 8.
#

set -u

SSSD_CONF="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf"
SSSD_CONF_DIR="/etc/sssd/conf.d"

# Ensure sssd is installed
if ! rpm -q sssd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "sssd package is not installed. Installing..."
    dnf install -y sssd || { echo "Failed to install sssd"; exit 1; }
fi

# Ensure the sssd.conf file exists
if [ ! -f "$SSSD_CONF" ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/sssd
    touch "$SSSD_CONF"
    chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"
    chmod 0600 "$SSSD_CONF"
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on sssd.conf
chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"
chmod 0600 "$SSSD_CONF"

# Ensure conf.d directory has proper perms if present
if [ -d "$SSSD_CONF_DIR" ]; then
    chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF_DIR"
    chmod 0700 "$SSSD_CONF_DIR"
fi

# Function to set a key=value under a given section in an ini-style file
set_ini_option() {
    local file="$1"
    local section="$2"
    local key="$3"
    local value="$4"

    # If the section is missing, append it with the setting
    if ! grep -qE "^\s*\[${section}\]" "$file"; then
        printf "\n[%s]\n%s = %s\n" "$section" "$key" "$value" >> "$file"
        return 0
    fi

    # Section exists. Check if the key exists within that section.
    awk -v section="[$section]" -v key="$key" -v value="$value" '
        BEGIN { in_sec=0; found=0; printed=0 }
        {
            # Detect section header lines
            if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*\[.*\][[:space:]]*$/) {
                # Leaving previous section: if it was our section and key not found, add it
                if (in_sec && !found) {
                    print key " = " value
                    found=1
                }
                if ($0 ~ ("^[[:space:]]*\\" section "[[:space:]]*$")) {
                    in_sec=1
                } else {
                    in_sec=0
                }
                print
                next
            }

            if (in_sec && $0 ~ ("^[[:space:]]*" key "[[:space:]]*=")) {
                print key " = " value
                found=1
                next
            }
            print
        }
        END {
            if (in_sec && !found) {
                print key " = " value
            }
        }
    ' "$file" > "${file}.tmp" && mv "${file}.tmp" "$file"

    chown root:root "$file"
    chmod 0600 "$file"
}

# Configure certificate_verification in the [sssd] section
# This enables OCSP checking for certificate-based authentication.
set_ini_option "$SSSD_CONF" "sssd" "certificate_verification" "ocsp_default_responder=, ocsp_default_responder_signing_cert="

# Actually the correct/simplest supported value is just to enable OCSP checking.
# Re-set to the standard hardened value.
set_ini_option "$SSSD_CONF" "sssd" "certificate_verification" "ocsp_dgst=sha1"

# Remove the placeholder line above and set to the required directive.
# The canonical STIG/CIS-approved setting is:
#   certificate_verification = ocsp_dgst=sha1
# but the primary requirement is that OCSP checking is NOT disabled
# (i.e. no_ocsp must not be present) and OCSP is enforced.
set_ini_option "$SSSD_CONF" "sssd" "certificate_verification" "ocsp_dgst=sha1"

# Ensure no drop-in file disables OCSP
if [ -d "$SSSD_CONF_DIR" ]; then
    for f in "$SSSD_CONF_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -qE '^\s*certificate_verification\s*=.*no_ocsp' "$f"; then
            sed -i -E 's/(^\s*certificate_verification\s*=.*),?\s*no_ocsp\s*,?/\1/' "$f"
        fi
        chown root:root "$f"
        chmod 0600 "$f"
    done
fi

# Sanity check: ensure no_ocsp is not present in main config
if grep -qE '^\s*certificate_verification\s*=.*no_ocsp' "$SSSD_CONF"; then
    sed -i -E 's/,?\s*no_ocsp//g' "$SSSD_CONF"
fi

# Validate final configuration
if grep -qE '^\s*certificate_verification\s*=' "$SSSD_CONF"; then
    echo "SSSD certificate_verification configured:"
    grep -E '^\s*certificate_verification\s*=' "$SSSD_CONF"
else
    echo "ERROR: certificate_verification not set in $SSSD_CONF" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Restart sssd to apply changes if it is enabled/running
if systemctl is-enabled --quiet sssd 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active --quiet sssd 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl restart sssd || echo "Warning: failed to restart sssd" >&2
fi

echo "OCSP-based certificate verification for SSSD has been enabled."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -u

FILE="/etc/security/opasswd"
KEY="identity"
RULE="-w ${FILE} -p wa -k ${KEY}"

# Ensure audit package files exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Function to add rule to a file if not present
add_rule() {
    local target="$1"
    if [ ! -f "$target" ]; then
        touch "$target"
    fi
    # Remove any existing rules for this file (with any key/perms) to avoid duplicates
    # Then append the correct rule
    if ! grep -Pq -- "^\s*-w\s+${FILE}\s+-p\s+wa\s+-k\s+${KEY}\s*$" "$target"; then
        # Remove any conflicting/partial rules for this file
        sed -i "\|^\s*-w\s\+${FILE}\s\+|d" "$target"
        echo "$RULE" >> "$target"
    fi
}

# Persistent rules for augenrules (rule-compilation program)
add_rule "/etc/audit/rules.d/identity.rules"

# Persistent rules for auditctl (direct rule-loading utility)
add_rule "/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Reload rules
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Pq -- "-w\s+${FILE}\s+-p\s+wa\s+-k\s+${KEY}"; then
        auditctl "$RULE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    # Check for uncommented line
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "$file"; then
        # Get current value

        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+.*|${key} ${value}|" "$file"

        echo "${key} ${value}" >> "$file"

}

fi

#!/bin/bash
# Remediation: Lock accounts after 3 failed login attempts on RHEL 8
# Uses authselect with faillock (standard on RHEL 8)

set -e

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
DENY_VALUE=3

# Ensure faillock.conf exists
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Set deny value in /etc/security/faillock.conf idempotently
if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*deny\s*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    sed -i -E "s|^\s*#?\s*deny\s*=.*|deny = ${DENY_VALUE}|" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
    echo "deny = ${DENY_VALUE}" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Ensure faillock is enabled via authselect
CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk -F': ' '/Profile ID/ {print $2}' | tr -d ' ')

if [ -z "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]; then
    # No profile selected; select sssd with faillock
    authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
else
    # Enable faillock feature on the current profile
    if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock
    fi
    authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify pam configuration references faillock
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [ -f "$pam_file" ]; then
        if ! grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "$pam_file"; then
            echo "Warning: pam_faillock.so not found in $pam_file" >&2
        fi
    fi
done

echo "Faillock configuration applied: deny=${DENY_VALUE}"
exit 0

#
# Remediation: Configure and lock the GDM login screen banner message.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.
#

set -u

BANNER_TEXT='You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only. By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions: -The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct (PM), law enforcement (LE), and counterintelligence (CI) investigations. -At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS. -Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose. -This IS includes security measures (e.g., authentication and access controls) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy. -Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details.'

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d"
DCONF_LOCK_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/gdm"
BANNER_KEYFILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/01-banner-message"
BANNER_LOCKFILE="${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}/01-banner-message"

# Ensure required packages are present (GDM/dconf).
if ! rpm -q dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y dconf || { echo "Failed to install dconf" >&2; exit 1; }
fi

# Create directories if missing.
install -d -m 0755 "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
install -d -m 0755 "${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}"
install -d -m 0755 "$(dirname "${DCONF_PROFILE}")"

# Ensure GDM dconf profile exists and is correct.
desired_profile=$'user-db:user\nsystem-db:gdm\nfile-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults\n'
if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${DCONF_PROFILE}")" != "${desired_profile%$'\n'}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${desired_profile}" > "${DCONF_PROFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${DCONF_PROFILE}"
fi

# Escape banner text for GVariant string literal (escape backslashes and single quotes).
escaped_banner=$(printf '%s' "${BANNER_TEXT}" | sed -e "s/\\\\/\\\\\\\\/g" -e "s/'/\\\\'/g")

# Write the banner keyfile.
new_keyfile_content="[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${escaped_banner}'
"

if [ ! -f "${BANNER_KEYFILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${BANNER_KEYFILE}")" != "${new_keyfile_content%$'\n'}" ] && [ "$(cat "${BANNER_KEYFILE}" 2>/dev/null)" != "${new_keyfile_content}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${new_keyfile_content}" > "${BANNER_KEYFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${BANNER_KEYFILE}"
fi

# Write the lock file to prevent user override.
new_lockfile_content="/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text
"

if [ ! -f "${BANNER_LOCKFILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${BANNER_LOCKFILE}")" != "${new_lockfile_content%$'\n'}" ] && [ "$(cat "${BANNER_LOCKFILE}" 2>/dev/null)" != "${new_lockfile_content}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${new_lockfile_content}" > "${BANNER_LOCKFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${BANNER_LOCKFILE}"
fi

# Update dconf database so changes take effect and persist.
if command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dconf update || { echo "dconf update failed" >&2; exit 1; }
else
    echo "dconf command not available" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "GDM login banner configured and locked successfully."
exit 0

set -euo pipefail

PARAM="net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts"
VALUE="1"
CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-icmp-broadcast.conf"

# Apply immediately
sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null

# Ensure persistence
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d

# Remove any existing setting for this parameter in the target file
if [ -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    sed -i "\|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=|d" "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

echo "${PARAM} = ${VALUE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"

# Remove conflicting settings in other common sysctl locations
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "${CONF_FILE}" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        if ! grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$f"; then
            sed -i "\|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=|d" "$f" || true
        fi
    fi
done

# Reload sysctl settings to ensure new config is active
sysctl --system >/dev/null

# Verify

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts"
VALUE="1"
CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-icmp-broadcast.conf"

# Apply immediately
sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null

# Ensure persistence
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d

# Remove any existing setting for this parameter in the target file
if [ -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    sed -i "\|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=|d" "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

echo "${PARAM} = ${VALUE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"

# Remove conflicting settings in other common sysctl locations
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "${CONF_FILE}" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        if ! grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$f"; then
            sed -i "\|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=|d" "$f" || true
        fi
    fi
done

# Reload sysctl settings to ensure new config is active
sysctl --system >/dev/null

# Verify
CURRENT=$(sysctl -n "${PARAM}")
if [ "${CURRENT}" != "${VALUE}" ]; then
    echo "Failed to set ${PARAM} to ${VALUE} (current: ${CURRENT})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "${PARAM} is set to ${VALUE} and persisted in ${CONF_FILE}"
```
